lines.st: You must be bored of nights like this.
The ones where the damp, cold fog creeps up on the roaring fires of the furnace. Ash permeates the air, its choking stench a constant assault on the nostrils.
Hard to imagine being able to sleep like this. Cramming a whole day's worth of work into an all-too-short dream, before awakening to find that things remain unchanged.
A black city, black stones, black clouds, and a blood-red flame ensconced in a furnace, unchanging since time immemorial.
No, this is not particularly conducive to a good night's sleep. At the very least, something should fill the dreary silence.
Something like...

lines.x: 9:30 P.M. \ Overcast
Kazdel, Residential District
The fire of the Soul Furnace~♪ Is Kazdel's sun truest~♪
It dries our childrens' clothes~♪ Warms our warriors' hearts~♪
(Stamps hoof to the beat)
Moo~ Moo~
There's no better place in Kazdel~♪ Than the plaza by the Furnace~♪ Those stingy little hawkers~♪ Haggle with some loser mercs~♪
Who the *Sarkaz swearing* do you think you're mouthing off about?! Your singing stinks! Going on all day like a broken horn.
We didn't lose to the Victorians! We had to—what were those again?
The fighters console themselves~♪ Calling it tactical retreat~♪
Right! It was a lactical defeat.
But even his burdenbeasts know~♪ His knapsack is now empty~♪ His daughter's not seen him in years~♪ Now~ looks~ at~ him~ funny~♪
The crowd warming by the furnace node begins to laugh.
Suddenly, an ear-piercing sound rends the air. The laughter abruptly stops.
Err... The warrior's an angry manglerbeast~♪ His hands, like claws, reach for my throat♪ Won't someone come to save me... (croak)♪
Oh, she will! Over here my savior comes—♪
The ceremony starts in five minutes! Knock it off! What did I say about putting your music sheets in the cordon, Snapstring? Get your clutter away from the Furnace already!
And you, Steelskull, back off from the cordon a bit. Unless you want your burdenbeast buddies to burn their fur?
Oh, it's you, Nymph. Joining in on the fun?
Relax, this broken furnace only spits out sparks. I'd know better than you, right? Settle down.
My head nearly got crushed~♪ But now I do recall~♪ I wanted to give the souls in the Furnace~♪ One last farewell song~♪
(Calmly warms itself by the fire)
I get it, not everyone's thirty-something like you. But things are about to change The Furnace used to burn revenants for power, but we're about to switch to a new source of energy.
Ermi says tonight's ceremony will be really, really important to get the revenants to come out. If something goes wrong and the Furnace goes 'boom'—
Steelskull! Are you even listening?
I saw much worse out there! Even the Feline's cannons barely even singed these guys, so what's a rinky-dink oven going to do, even if it blows?
The fire's just right tonight. Might as well get my blade reforged.
(Harrumphs happily)
Why... won't you guys just listen?
Fine! I'll do it myself!
Hey, don't tug on my burdenbeasts! They—
Waahh!
They'll think you're playing with them and knock you into the dirt.
The unreasonable is now reasonable~♪ You bully so you don't get bullied~♪ Our ancestors've seen enough~♪ They're about to go flying free~♪
That's dumb, Sarkaz don't bully Sarkaz.
(Disdainful whistle)
Ow... That hurt...
The plaza's packed. Are you really going to tell everyone to go home one by one? You'll need more than one set of legs.
Can't you just, I dunno, use your Djall powers to scare them all off in one go?
No, nuh-uh! That's simply out of line! Sarkaz don't bully Sarkaz, remember? Also, you should never, ever treat a Djall's powers so lightly!
Okay okay, no wonder you're the kindest Djall... no, the nicest Sarkaz in all of Kazdel, Nymph. Now go break a leg.
Nymph looks behind her at the furnace node plaza, filled to the brim with raucous singing, peddlers hawking their wares, and hammers beating on weapons.
In the distance, the Soul Furnace towers above, belching out an acrid, thick black smoke. A careful listener could hear the faint screeching of the revenants.
For the past two hundred years, day after day, it has sent energy to the furnace nodes, which then pass that energy on to the countless homes in Kazdel.
I don't know what will happen to the revenants after the ceremony... Come to think of it, I don't know what a revenant even looks like.
*sigh* I promised Ermi I'd make sure the people in this neighborhood were safe before the ceremony started.
Why can't the Sarkaz in Kazdel just do as they're told...
What is the meaning of this? Are you going to let me finish reading or not?
Another fit? Oh good, why don't you milk this out all night? It's not as if you're the one paying for the ceremony, are you?
Enough. I don't like being here and inhaling these noxious gases either. If you could all be a tad bit more cooperative, we could get this over with and be on our separate ways.
I shall just carry on from where I was interrupted, not that any of you are listening in the first place.
'The duty of the Soul Furnace nears a close, the torments of thine burning body hereto shall end.'
'Thus under the eyes of the myriad souls do I pray...'
If I could offer a comment, though the ceremonial recitation states 'pray', you ought to know that this is more of a... notice.

Also, 'Await these rites in peace, spread not thine suffering' means 'pipe down and don't cause a ruckus'. Things are rather hectic in the city right now, and if you could just wait until I have a moment spare, I'll give you all the pomp and circumstance you need.
When the time comes, do as you will, whether that means going home or staying here to help. Hell, get into a fight, if that's what you want. Just don't tear down the city walls while you're at it.
Did you get all that? Speak up if you did.

lines.st: Your rites mean nothing... We have waited and suffered for too long!
You threw us into this furnace...
Too long! The burning! The scorching!

lines.x: Yes, yes, I understand you've been through a lot. Just to be fair, I didn't throw you in there, and we the Sarkaz today are not ungrateful.
What we're doing at this very moment is trying to get you guys out. So pipe down already!

lines.st: Millions upon millions of nights... Traveling through the dark, my kindreds, my screams, have been drowned!
Young Lich! A mere child—
Insincere! Insolent! Ignorant!
Still your tongue, cease your lies, and get thee gone!

lines.x: *Sarkaz swearing* You better do as you're told if you know what's good for you! I've tried to keep this civilized!
Act your age already, you're over ten thousand years old—
As the wizened Lich rants and raves, a stray glob of slag splashes across his face.
Master! Is everything alright?
The gauge indicates that the Furnace's energy output isn't stable. Is something the matter with the ceremony? Do you require my assistance?
*cough* *cough*... *cough*!
Stay back! You're not needed yet!
*tirade of Sarkaz swearings* Mark my words, if these geriatric fools aren't taken care of by tonight, I'll be Manfred's personal shoeshine!
*cough* *cough*... *cough*!
General, you've been standing guard all day. You should take a rest.
Now's the pivotal moment of the ceremony. Nothing can be allowed to go wrong.
Take your positions. If you notice anything out of the ordinary, report to me at once.
Yes, sir!
Hmm...
It seems Kazdel is still somewhat chilly this time of year.
Hey, you really ready to meet your old pals?
Today is a crossroads... The end of an endless history.
Their sacrifice may not have been willing, but it was their agony and torment that fueled Kazdel for thousands of years. For that, they have my utmost respect.
Not willing? You were the one who caught them and threw them into the Furnace in the first place. You really think you'll manage a nice, peaceful chat with them?
Her Highness has restored my sanity and given me clarity. My mind is no longer shrouded in haze, my heart no longer gripped by senseless rage.
Let me see them. I'll try to quell their anger. It was I who began this age of suffering. It is only fitting that I end it.
Hmph...
Here's hoping you can.
Their souls are roasting in the Furnace~♪ They've got emotions of every sort~♪ Will they really be ready to wave goodbye~♪ To their old metal home~♪
Hey, you! Stop gawping at the Furnace like an idiot and start gawping at my wares like a genius! If you don't like 'em, you've got no taste! Come take a look, girl... Oh, you again?
Fatwallet! About last time—
Oh no, are you hurt? You should watch where you're running.
The girl makes a face and sticks her tongue out at Nymph before peeling off into the distance.
While you're short of customers, Fatwallet, you need to get your stuff outside the cordon. I can help.
Eh, fine by me. Not like I've got any customers.

Really?
That's great! I'll get started, then.
Gonna need payment first though. If I have to move my whole stall, then you're gonna have to rent the spot from me.
R-Rent? But this is just an empty spot by the road...

Well, it's mine now. It's a good spot by the Furnace, lots of slackers hanging about. It's warm, too. You can get your three squares a day here, without having to waste energy at home. It won't be cheap.
Anyway, that's enough free talk. You want anything more out of me, it's gonna cost ya. You're getting in the way of business here.
Hey—
Nymph looks to the Soul Furnace with worry. It might be her imagination, but the smoke appears to be getting thicker.
A job only I can do?
Sure, Ermi! My purse has been pretty light lately, so this is a lifesaver! What do you need from me? Repairs? Looking after burdenbeasts? I can do a little bit of everything.
Oh, I would never task you with something so trivial, o kind and considerate Nymph. It'd be a terrible waste of talent.
Let me think... What should this position be called... Neighborhood... Officer?
Neighborhood Officer?
Alright, I may have just come up with that title. But it's really important! You'll be the friendly neighborhood solver of any tricky issues.
As you know, ever since that Victorian business, we've been dreadfully spread thin, and maintaining order in the streets is becoming more difficult by the day.
But when it comes to making sure everyone's safe, you're one of the best. You're Kazdel's most considerate Djall, after all.
I... I am?
But... Okay! I get the idea!
Make sure everyone's safe... Just leave it to me, Ermi! I'll do my very best!
Fatwallet, if I rent this spot from you, you'll move outside the cordon, right? That's what you said. Let me see, my...
Where's my purse?!
Wait, it's definitely on me somewhere... I must have put it in another pocket... How could this be...? Maybe I forgot to bring it with me when I left the house...?
Looks like I've got a fool on my hands. Maybe you're a nice, honest soul? Nope, there's no such thing. So a fool you must be.
My purse is gone... And the ceremony's almost over now...
Fatwallet! Please please please move your stall away, please? If you don't move, then Steelskull won't either, then everyone's going to get burned by the Furnace.
Ugh, that big lummox still holding a grudge? All I did was store my goods in his house while he was out fighting. Not like he said I couldn't.
I looked after that daughter of his for years! Him? He slinks back from Victoria, tail between his legs, completely empty-handed. You think I babysit for free?!
You looked after her? You raised her to be a *Sarkaz swearing* thief! I gave her a name so I could send her off to learn to read and write! And you ruined all that!

Wait, please stop fighting. The Furnace... the cordon... there's no time!
Ah! That's my purse! Please don't run—
Ow!
Nymph is tripped up by the girl and lands face-first in a puddle of mud. Bystanders chuckle as they help her up, and covered from head to toe in muck, she glances dejectedly at the quarreling duo.
Everywhere she looks, people are going about their business as usual within the cordon. Even the pushover wandering singer is sitting cross-legged, composing a new song by the light of the furnace.
Oh what to do, what to do~♪ What she wants~ Is really simple~♪♪ But now she finds herself~ In a pickle♪
You done yet? What's taking so long? Wasn't the last few times real smooth?
If you can't hack it, just step down and let me. Hey—
*sigh* Who even let you up here, Wiš'adel? Did I not instruct Ermengarde to keep everyone away from the ceremony?
It's bad enough that you're here, did you really have to bring that one along too?
You've been floating up there forever and nothing's happened. I came because I was worried about you!
You came because you wanted to rubberneck! As for being worried about me, best you worry about yourself.
What's to worry about? I wasn't the one who stuffed our ancestors in the fire to roast like sweet potatoes.
Good. Those who dare speak to a revenant in such a manner tend to meet an unhappy end.

lines.st: The murderer... returns.
Is it him? He must... pay! MAKE HIM PAY!
Pain, agony, forsaken... all his debts! How dare he...
Silence, all of you! Listen, to what he has to say!

lines.x: Debt? I owe you nothing.
It was all... a necessary price to pay.

lines.st: Then why did you not stay?
Then why do you not throw them in too?
Then why did you return?!

lines.x: I am here to pick you up.
Your duty is at an end.
I've come to welcome you, as you leave this cramped purgatory.

lines.st: Ridiculous! Laughable!
It was you who threw us in here! You who left alone!
How dare you? HOW DARE YOU?
How dare you return? So brazen and condescending?
I will see you pay the price! I will see everything burn in the raging flames of this furnace as we did!

lines.x: I understand your anger. I will face your wrath.
But now, I just want you to come up and see the Kazdel today.
Come and see. See the city you've devoted yourselves to. It is proof that you lived—

lines.st: You have no place to say that to us!
No, wait... You... the one we called the eldest of revenants...
Why have you become like... this?

lines.x: Hah...
You need not feel sorrow for me.
My shattered soul has been burned away. Nothing is left.

lines.st: Eldest of revenants... Why...?
Why are you in a cannon?
No, it's more like a Sankta gun.
Our vaunted elder, trapped inside a Sankta gun.
Haha... Hahahaha!
Hahahahaha...

lines.x: What're they arguing about, old man? Translate for me.
Hmm... I'm not entirely sure it could be called an argument.

Huh? Why so quiet all of a sudden? They hashed things out already?
Pffeh... Pffh! Maybe don't blast ash in your guest's face?
What the hell was that?
Based on my prior dealings with these fellows, that would be the equivalent of spitting in your face.
Me? What did I do?!
*Sarkaz swearing*! Hey, you old farts! What gives?!
I... This...
You... are all...
BEYOND REASONING!
What's going on?
General! Our cannons! They've started charging on their own! I think it's the remnants of the revenant fragment in the core, it's—
Starting up our guns, is it? What's the target?
If I'm reading this right... It's the Soul Furnace!
Quick! Cut all power—
Fantastical lights explode and streak across the night sky like a rain of fire.
Cold, weary, and unchanging as ever, Kazdel silently absorbs their fiery descent.
There is always someone awake, yearning for a glimpse of something different from the small patch of night sky outside their window.
Something like... a sudden, violent burst of fireworks.
The Furnace!
Who! Fired! That! Cannon?!
My roof! Darn it, I just fixed it up a few days ago. Where'd that big rock even come from...? And what's that burning smell?
Ow! My head... hot hot hot!

lines.st: Dang it...

lines.x: Eh? Wh-What was that?
No, I don't have time to worry about the roof...
Everyone, please listen! That accident is definitely connected to the ceremony. The Soul Furnace could restart at any moment. For your safety, please evacuate outside the cordon immediately!
Clang! Clang! Clang! The mercenary mending his weapon keeps hammering away without even looking up.
Come one, come all, come and take a look. You're blind if you don't buy anything—
You... You all really...!

lines.st: Just *Ancient Sarkaz swearing* die already.

lines.x: Steelskull's hammer stops mid-swing, Fatwallet's shouting stops mid-sentence, and the wandering singer's guitar string snaps mid-pluck.
Girl... What did you just say?
I said!

lines.st: Are you deaf?

lines.x: Wha... What?
For the sake of everyone's safety...

lines.st: Heheheh...
Burn—burn—I'll burn you all into ashes—

lines.x: H-Hey, calm down... What're you coming over here for? My clothes and the burdenbeasts are fla—err, what was it—flammable!
He's right, don't do anything crazy!
Get behind the cordon now, or else...

lines.st: Cowards who flee from battle!
Thieves who steal from their own!
Lowlifes who indulge in pleasure!
Idlers who shirk their duties!
I'll kill you all! Kill! KILL!

lines.x:
Or else...
Huh? Where'd everyone go?

One by one, the plaza's crowd turned their heads, staring at Nymph in disbelief. Steelskull frantically tries to drag his burdenbeasts out of the cordon, while Fatwallet packs up her stall with surprising speed for someone her age.
Nymph feels something bump into her belly. The girl who stole her purse shoves it back into her hands, then runs off as fast as she can.
The wandering singer now stands meekly outside the cordoned area with his sheets under his arm, as if he has been there the whole time.
Wow, that's...
Nymph scratches her head, setting free the glimmering fragment that had fallen into her hair. It still retains the heat of the Furnace it was so unduly ejected from.
As it tumbles out of her hair, the strange voice in her head cuts off.
Nymph! Can you hear me?
Nymph! Nymphy! Hey!
Is that you, Ermi? How come I can't see you?
Look at the mirror in the little box I gave you.
Wow, Ermi, how'd you get inside the mirror?
Didn't I tell you when I gave it to you? You must have missed the instructions I wrote again.
Lich creations aren't so easy to understand. Besides, if I ever need to use it, you'll help me.
So, what's going on? I saw a really big explosion over by the Furnace!
Something went awry at the ceremony, and now the ancestors are throwing a fit. Get this... the revenant on the airship got into a fight with those in the Furnace!
Are you okay?
I'm fine, a little explosion like that couldn't hurt me. My master's still counting on me to run errands for him, though I have a bit more on my plate than I can handle.
As for the Furnace... It's still intact, and there haven't been any further issues. But the explosion caused another problem, and I miiiiight have to ask you for help again!
I'm just glad you're alright. Whatever you need help with, just say the word.
We need to hunt down all the bits of slag that got ejected from the Furnace and see if they have any revenant fragments in them.
Revenant... fragments? Are they dangerous? A lot of people here have been picking up shards of stuff that fell from the sky after the explosion.
They are! Which is why master wants me to find them as quickly as possible. The revenants in the Furnace are restless now that they're missing some of their kind.
As for the revenant fragments that are scattered, they're like ticking time bombs. We don't know when they're going to stir up trouble.
But don't worry too much. Judging by the number of revenants still inside the Furnace, only a handful of fragments have a revenant riding along. Fewer than ten, in fact.
*phew* Finally, some good news. I was thinking about how long it'd take to find every single bit that fell from the sky.
Well, unfortunately, that's the difficult part of all this. We have no idea where these fragments fell. They could be anywhere in Kazdel, really.
So, what do the ones with the revenants look like?

To be honest, I haven't had a proper look at one either. It shouldn't be too hard to identify something with a revenant riding inside it, though. It'll influence the emotions and senses of the people around it.
Would they happen to be surrounded by wisps of smoke? Maybe they sparkle and flash too?
Yeah! Something like that!
Then you might want to look at this and see if it's what you're looking for... Though I haven't felt it influence my emotions or anything.
Are you at home? I'll head over right now. Whatever you do, don't give that thing to anyone else. We're lucky you're a Djall!
Ah, okay! I'm by the furnace node near my house, so come as quick as you can. Hmm... This little rock sure is hot.
Ermi, you're back! What did your master say?
The good news is that it really was a revenant fragment. Master's already placed it back in the Furnace. The bad news is that we might have been too quick with retrieving it.
Why's that a bad thing? Shouldn't we be putting them back right away?
Wait, did your master... compliment you?
Even you have him figured out by now. Yeah, he did, and every time he does, he's almost always building up to bad news!

You know how I have to inspect goods going in and out of Kazdel every day, organize patrols, arrange repairs for the holes in the Furnace, and even inventory all the stuff he brought back from Leithanien, right?
He's already running me ragged, and now he wants me to...
Ermi! Did you try telling him that you're already swamped and that someone else should go gather the fragments?
I did tell him that! I even told him the only reason we found this first one so quickly was you.
And then? Did he change his mind?
Why are you looking at me like that... Wait, Ermi! Hey—!
Nymph~
Help me out again, last time I swear. I really have too much to handle and that old tool doesn't really care. Please, you're the only one I can count on now...
But...
You just need to find all the fragments and bring them back. I'll give you a nice, big reward if you do.
I know you don't really lack anything, but I guarantee it'll be something you really want. And... it's something only I can give you, not even my master could.
Well, don't keep me guessing. Just say what it is and I'll think it over.
I can't tell you too much at the moment, but don't you worry, we Liches are a generous people.
Hmph, is that really the proper attitude to ask for help? Anyone else would have walked away, but not this silly Djall. She just lets you walk all over her.
So fine, I'll help you find these fragments, but do you have a map that can show where they are, Ermi? The kind with the little black dots floating around?
Do you think Liches can just conjure things from thin air? Why would I have something so strange?
But you have a point, you'd be chasing your own tail if I just sent you off to find the fragments with no help.
Not to mention that you don't have anything that could actually contain the revenant fragments... Pass me that box thing of yours while I think for a second.
I'll need some time to give this a few upgrades. Head home and rest for now. Once I'm done, I'll come find you.
Eh? I thought this was a super urgent task.
Oh, I wouldn't go that far. And once you have the right tool, you'll save far more time than the few hours it'll take me to make it.
Fine, but I still hope you can think over the map thing a bit more. I'll put my box over here. See you later, Ermi!
A way to detect revenants... To isolate them... What if I dismantled this part...? No, that won't work...
Ermi? I'm heading back now. I'll wait for your good news!
Eh? Oh, alright, bye-bye!
Liches are always so quick to jump into thinking mode. I really hope she doesn't become like her master one day...
*yawn* I've got important things to do tomorrow, so I'd better get some rest.
I wonder how many people will dream about those fireworks tonight?
*pant* *pant* It's still so hot... like trying to hold a roast sweet potato fresh out of the oven. Can't imagine how hot it must be in the Furnace.

It's nice to meet you... ancestor sir? I don't quite know what to call you. You must have been burning in the Furnace since my grandparents were still little. You really gave it good to that crowd just now.
Nymph walks over to a quiet corner of the plaza and sits down on some steps.
I should probably put you back where you belong, but getting to meet you was something really special... I'd better tell you what I need to say quick.
I've always known you were real, ever since I was young. Everyone said you were just stories to scare little kids, but I always believed.
Back then, there was a Babel camp right by my doorstep. Mom and Dad never let me wander too far, so I was always looking for the camp's doctors to play with.
My favorite fairy tale was the future the Babel doctors told me about.
And then... My Babel friends left without warning. One day I'd go look for them as usual, and there was just the field.

I was really sad, so I cried and cried at the furnace node in the plaza, because everyone told me the underground pipes connected to it would carry my voice all the way to the great, big Soul Furnace.
Now that I've seen, or heard you, I feel so happy. You really do exist and you've been protecting us and all of Kazdel this whole time and—

lines.st: Noisy.

lines.x: Sorry!
Nymph's eyes widen as a shadow slowly seeps out of the shimmering slag. It is barely a wisp, as faint as a cicada's wings, but at least he doesn't sound as angry as before.
Shabby.
You mean our neighborhood? Haha, well, I think it's kinda nice, once you get used to it.
Barely any improvement.

Improvement? Oh, you mean compared to two hundred years ago.
Right... You've been in the Furnace all that time. Maybe this is your first time out.
Nymph stretches out her hand that the slag is resting on, allowing him a clearer view of the plaza. The first view he's had in two hundred years that did not include flames.
See, ancestor revenant sir? This is where we live nowadays.
What's that sound?
Snapstring's singing. That instrument he's holding is cobbled together from scraps. It looks like a guitar, but I don't know what it's actually called.
And the mercenary?
You mean Steelskull? He's had a few drinks, and now he's dancing around his burdenbeast battle buddies.
What of the army?
Uh... Do you mean the Military Commission? Maybe, umm... Do you see that big airship flying overhead? I've heard they're up there.
Rest—
After a long period of silence, that is the only word the slag says. Nymph doesn't quite understand the meaning.
Not too far away, the wandering singer plucks at his instrument and begins to sing a song of farewell, as promised.

eps.name: The Streets on the Way Back

lines.x: I came to you to help some big shots solve your problems. You won't find a shoddier district in all Kazdel.
I have a group of real good workers willing to help build a furnace node. Think about it: with just a supply of power, this district will be able to transform.
People will have work to keep themselves fed and won't cause trouble anymore.
You sound plenty confident. What do you want from me?
Steel for the pipes, right in the middle of the district. You can have your people keep an eye on it, if you want.
Trust me, you won't recognize this district in three months.
You see those guards watching over the steel? Do you believe me now? It's the bosses who ordered the furnace node to be built. There's plenty in it for you, if you work for them.
Hmph. And?
I want food, enough to feed a decent team of workers. I know you guys have plenty.
Bring it to the warehouse there. We'll have enough space.
Trust me, this is a good deal for you.

You see the food in this warehouse? Come help build the furnace, and everyone gets some every day. Better than robbing people on the streets. At least you don't have to worry about getting your skull split open.
You've got guts, kid. You want to hire mercenaries to work for you?
You're big, tough, and not afraid to break a sweat. You'll make great workers.
Listen to me, and I'll make sure you all do well for yourselves—better than you're doing right now, at least.
7:40 A.M. \ Overcast
Good morning, Big Head! Came to get your clothes? Your collar would've been charred, if you came any later.
Don't you dare call me Big Head. And put away all that crap you picked up. Don't *Sarkaz expletive* pile shit up in front of my door!
Just one night. I'm selling it to the boiler guy later. Won't bother you, not that much, anyway.
The hoodlums would have stolen them if I left them at home. But no one in these parts would dare have their finger in your pie.
You're talkative today. Aren't you going to work?
Didn't you hear that nasty noise last night? A rock THIS big fell right on top of the construction site.
If I went to work now, I'd have to help move the rock and fill in the crater... why would I want to do three times the work for the same amount of food? Say, you're up early, are you going somewhere?
No, just in the mood for a morning stroll.
Well, don't go to the south side. It's a mess.
I was out there cutting wires in the morning when I heard that a hunk of iron fell from the sky, straight through somebody's roof. They hardly started grinning when the Broken Horns showed up.
Now there's a handful of gangs wanting to trade that iron for food. Everybody wants a big piece and they're almost fighting over it.
Over iron? Is it valuable?
Oriron isn't valuable only when burdenbeasts fly.
Those Broken Horn hardheads would draw their blades for half a hunk of black brick. They'd've torn the house down if the Consultant wasn't there to keep them in line.
Well, they'll stay in line with the Consultant watching them.
Don't say I didn't warn you. And I would take the long way round, if I were you. You see those two over there?
Where?
You know Nymph. Don't know the other one, but she looks like a Lich.
Those two were keeping order around here before you came back.
A Lich? What are they doing here? Isn't she worried about getting her nice shoes dirty?
Might be Nymphy's friend. Look how they're laughing with each other.
There's the boiler man! Gotta borrow your cart, Big Head.
What did you just say?
I-I mean, Big Guy! Have a nice stroll, I've got junk to sell.
I'll let it slide this time... if you share half the money you made.
Huh? Now just wait a second, Big Guy, (*whispers*) it's not like I don't want to share with you, but what's money good for in Kazdel these days?
If a revenant fragment is too big, press this red button in the back until the box becomes big enough to hold it.
And do NOT poke your head inside, no matter what happens. That's pretty much it. There's a manual inside as well. Any questions?
You really don't have a treasure map that can show revenant fragment locations in real time?
I told you, Liches don't grant wishes! Making Arts Creations is serious business.
Here, put this on.
The Lich Arts inside will detect nearby revenant fragments, and emit an alarm when you're close enough.
The box looks like a mercenary communicator.
Be glad you got that. I was planning to make the case out of scrap paper.

But this doesn't look much more serious than a treasure map.
Hey, I was joking! Give it back!
I have to keep an eye on the furnace repairs, and review the books that my master gave me. I'm counting on you to find the revenant fragments, Nymph.
Of course! That's what friends are for.
They were right! You're the kindest, gentlest Djall in all Kazdel!
Wh-Who says? There aren't that many Djaj in Kazdel. I'm just helping out a friend.
Well, you get back to work. I-I got to go myself!
Are you sure? Is this where you saw the meteor fall?
Yes, this is the place.
But Ermi's detector isn't reacting at all... do you remember what the meteor looked like?
Some of them were round, some of them bumpy...
There was more than one?
The wonderful smell when roasted on an open flame...
What?
Leave the skin on. Soft and fluffy... simply lip-smacking.
Ma'am, what are you talking about?
Go inside. They're only open in the morning, and they won't stay open for you.
I'm looking for the meteor, not Mercenary Potatoes! The stuff that the fireworks dropped last night!
Fried rice? There's no fried rice.

Never mind... I guess I might as well ask the mercenaries.
Get some rest, granny, I'll bring roast potatoes next time.
Thanks. I like mine a little charred.
Nymph keeps walking. The courtyard is not big, but there are a lot of things here.
Mercenaries who back from the front lines have set up camp. Simple ovens bake in a corner, with embers still smoldering inside.
Hello... anybody here?
You're too late.
Wh-What?
I bought the last bag of roast potatoes. Come back tomorrow.
I'm not here for potatoes!
We've met twice before, but that was during the civil war. I guess you don't remember.
The roast potatoes are pretty good. I could give you half a one, if you want.
No thanks... I'm here for the meteor that fell last night.
You're after that piece of iron? Well, it's not here anymore.
The Broken Horns didn't get what they wanted at the Veteran's Home, so they got a few gangs together to bring it to their own turf.
The commotion lasted all morning, and nobody's mended that hole in the roof.
You said a few gangs? Did they all touch that... piece of iron?
A dozen or so folks must have touched it, maybe a hundred. A few wise guy tried to scrape some Oriron off with their fingernails.
Are you shivering? Do you need a potato to warm your hands?
Oh, er, I'm fine.
Go on, don't be shy, I can spare that much.
Thanks... you said they went to all that trouble for a rock with some iron. Doesn't that sound a little weird?
You think so too? The young Broken Horns seemed really obsessed with the iron. They just wouldn't leave it alone.
You have any idea what's going on?
Let me think...
Oh, why am I asking you? It's not like you live here.
Yeah, I haven't been to this district in years. I don't know it well... where can I find the Broken Horns?
Go straight until the end of the path, turn right, and keep walking until you reach Burningtail Junction, where the cherry trees used to be. You'll see it.
Thanks! Say, what's your name?

They call me...
Well, you can call me Pohl.
I'm Nymph!
There is a sudden crack of thunder in the dark sky, but no raindrops fall.
The sound echoes through the walls, rubble and buried pipelines. The thick and heavy shell begins to peel off, revealing the original texture beneath.
Oh dear, look at the time! It's been a pleasure talking to you, but I have to get some veggies from the Vertical Farm. Gotta rush, bye!
Oh, okay! Bye!
The young Sarkaz carries his shopping bag with his tail and strolls leisurely into the alley, showing no signs of a rush.
Burningtail Junction... cherry tree? I remember the place, but I thought the cherry trees were cut down before the civil war.
I smell fried potatoes. Where are they?
Here were are. This street still looks the same.
There used to be a huge piece of Originium crystal here. Did they move it out, or do I remember wrong?
Bean Sprout said things were bad here, but I haven't seen a single hoodlum yet. I should sock him one when I get back.
My house is just around the corner. She used to stand guard here, and I'd get a good beating if she caught me running out into the streets.
What's the point of looking for it, though? It's been so many years. People come and go; somebody else probably lives there now.
What would they think if I said it used to be my home? They'd probably think I was crazy, or just an idiot. Not like I can come back and reclaim it as mine.
...Just a quick look. That'll do.
Go around the corner and get your look. Then go get something to eat. Just another day.
Wait, where's the house?
Just a wall? Where's the roof?
Who tore down my house?!
What are you shouting about, silly big guy?
Shouldn't you go home and roast some potatoes? What are you doing on Broken Horn turf? They'll strip you naked, you know.
Whose kid are you? Who taught you to talk like that?
The girl in the window does not answer. She makes a face and wags her little head.
The mercenary sees the black rocks growing out of her neck.
This is Broken Horn turf? How?
Don't you see the red words on the wall? Oh, don't tell me—you can't read?
It says, 'TRAITOR'. That's what they say happens to the guys who get on the wrong side of the Broken Horns.
Hey, don't go! I can teach you, if you want to learn to read. I've got nothing against you Head-Sellers. Hehe.
The mercenary stops in his tracks and turns around, looking up at the girl from below the window.
His yellow, muddled eyes make it difficult to hide much emotion.
What's a Head-Seller?
That's what the Broken Horns call people like you, wearing a jacket like that, coming back from the outside.
But the girl who taught me to read won't let me use that word. She says...
Where can I find the Broken Horns?
Hey, stop right there! Who are you?
Two days in the Broken Horns got you blind? I'm here for the gang meeting.
Gang meeting? You? What are you talking about? This isn't your barbecue stall. Since when do cooks get a seat at the table?
Has your dumb ass healed up? You want me to get the Consultant?
I don't see him anywhere. Where are you gonna find him? Boss says the meeting starts after three bangs on the steel bucket, and no one else gets to go in.
I'm not deaf. It's only been two bangs.
Oh?
That's three there.
Heh, you know what, I'll take a little pity on you. You want to go in, give me three roast potatoes tomorrow, and I'm not paying for them.
Sure. If you dare.
Take a guess what extra seasoning I'm gonna put on them.
Hey, hold it! Did I say you could go in?
What are you looking at? I'm talking to you!
Me? I'm here to listen...
Get lost! This is no place for you. See this?
Er... a fire poker?
A knife! Made from a horn! It's really sharp!
Ouch! That must have hurt. Why would you poke your finger like that? Here, I have a bandage...
You—get lost!
This should be the place, but how do I get in?
I've heard that the Broken Horns are a bunch of unruly young kids, and now...
Come on, Nymph. Think of your friends. Could anyone help?
Not this one, not that one either. This one might have some influence at a gang meeting, but I don't think she's in Kazdel anymore...
What about the Consultant? All the gangs in this district listen to him. But I've never seen him, and I don't know where he lives.
I should just wait here. He's always the one officiating the gang meetings. I'm sure he'll come by.
...No one's coming?!
Are you waiting for someone, Miss Nymph?
Pohl! Did you get your veggies?
I was a little late. All they had left was sweet potatoes. That farmer's a grumpy one, he doesn't let you pick. It took a lot of sweet-talking to get a few decent-looking ones.
Wow, sweet potatoes can get this big? The ones I know aren't even a third the size.
The only way to get ones this big is to try your luck at the Vertical Farm, but I hear the farmers keep the best ones for themselves.
Anyway, what are you doing here?
Oh, I was trying my luck to see if I could run into the Consultant here. The Broken Horns won't let me in, but they wouldn't stop me if I was with the Consultant.
It might not go down quite like that... do you know the Consultant?
I asked someone to help get in touch with him, but I never got a response.
So you're waiting for someone you've never met? How would you recognize him?
That's why I said I was trying my luck. Maybe he'd know me?
Is that piece of iron really so important?
Not just to me. It's important to everyone else here. I have to bring it back to—
The Furnace?
Yes! You saw it too? The fireworks last night...
They came from the Furnace. But wasn't the appeasement ceremony in progress then? Are we talking about... revenants? But why are you here? What about the Liches?
Huh? You know about all that?
Just my luck, I thought I was imagining things... this is even worse than I thought.
You've been standing here for a while, right? How many times did they bang the steel bucket?
Three times, I think.
The gang meeting has started. I shouldn't have spent so much time shopping today.
It may not be too late. I think they'll wait for the Consultant...

Fair point. A gang meeting without the Consultant is basically a brawl.
Hmm...
Well, it's your lucky day, Nymph. Or maybe unlucky day. Come on, let's get this trouble all fixed up.
Huh? No, wait! You don't even know what's going on...
I'll tell you my theory on the way, and you can correct me, if I'm wrong.
Beastshit! No goddamned way!
That courtyard was given to us by the Military Commission, and the Consultant signed off on all business on these streets! Why should we move out just because you say so?
Calm down, Barrel, this isn't personal. As for you, Beck, you need to watch your words.
As if I'm wrong, Ebony?
Why should these Head-Sellers be allowed to take our turf after spending so many years on the outside?
You weren't the boss of your gang when the Consultant gave them those two blocks.
You know what, I should have kicked the old man off his seat sooner. I wouldn't have let the Head-Sellers set foot in here if I was in the room back then.
Say that again, I dare you.
Don't just stand there staring, pull them apart! You should get your mouth slapped, Beck. They were out there risking their lives for us.
And you, Barrel, stop being such a hard-head! Beck could run his mouth all day, but none of it matters until the Consultant gets here.
Stop defending them, Ebony! Who gives a damn about the Consultant? How many men does he have? How many blades does he have?
Not as many men as you. But blades? That's hard to say.
Consultant.
Consultant!
Consultant?!
Who's the Djall?

Oh, don't mind me, I'm just...
...I'm the Consultant's assistant! I'm here to help him take minutes.
Yeah, my wrist's been hurting lately. You could take her place, if you have problem with it.
Oh, I forgot, you can't read.
Well, everyone's here. Let's get started... unless someone has something important to do.
Not at all. We're all waiting on you to decide how to split the piece of iron.
The cloth over it is pulled away, stirring up a cloud of dust. Nymph covers her nose with the back of her hand and squints at what lay underneath.
It is a large rock that has been touched by fire and smoke, with broken Originium pieces scattered across the surface. Underneath the cracked exterior are honeycomb-shaped rust marks.
The woman, whom the others called Ebony, takes out a screwdriver and pries a small piece of rust from the crack. Light shines through the smoke upon it, reflecting a faint blue glow.
The device on Nymph's wrist trembles, as though it just felt a chill.
Looks like there's a fair bit of Oriron. Is this it?
I think so...
(*whispers*) But Ermi said the detector would sound an alarm. Why is it quiet?
The Oriron extracted from a piece this big could feed you all for a month. But who's going to extract it? All these little Originium bits on it are like mini-bombs, if not properly processed.
Some of my guys had an ironworking shop on the Columbian frontier.
You'll roast it like you roast your potatoes?
Shut the *Sarkaz expletive* up if you don't know what you're talking about. I can always cut it up before processing it.
That won't work. The Originium fragments are unstable. They could go off at any time.
We've done business with the furnace node manager over by the western wall. Should we ask him?
Didn't you understand? The fragments are unstable. You think he would risk blowing up his furnace?
Don't forget where the fireworks last night came from. If the big shots catch you, whether it's the Military Commission—
Or the Liches, who have a slightly grim opinion of you fellas—
You know what's gonna happen. Not my problem if it's just your own graves that you're digging, but not when you might leave the rest of us starving.
Is there no place in Kazdel that would take it? You're not telling us to send it back where it came from, are you?
You're getting ahead of yourself, Ebony. We haven't gotten the Oriron out yet. Besides, just because no one will take it in Kazdel doesn't mean no one will take it out there.
Some smugglers will do anything, no matter how dangerous. You might take a hefty haircut on it, but it's better than leaving it to rot in your hands.
How big of a haircut are we talking?
How should I know? It's not my area of expertise. This is what I do as a consultant: I tell you how to cut the cake fairly, but I'm never the one to actually cut it.
How can we be sure the difference won't end up in your pocket?
What are you saying, Beck? Apologize to the Consultant, now!
To him? I don't understand why you're so polite with this guy. Have things been peaceful for so long that you forgot where you should be pointing your blade?
I say he's in league with the noble masters up in the towers. He makes it so we don't get to fight or eat, so we end up fixing the walls for them!
Why would he help the Head-Sellers take my turf? I've lived in Kazdel since the day I was born. Why should I give my house to some slugs who haven't set foot here in decades?
Draw your blade. Nobody's gonna say I killed an unarmed man.
*Sarkaz expletive*, give it a rest, both of you! You can fight it out yourselves once we've got the food.
Put your blades down, NOW!

Ahem. I guess someone forgot what my job is.
I just give advice. You decide to take it or not.
I know a few smugglers, but let's just say it'd take some credit for them to handle such a hot potato.
Sorry about Beck, Consultant. He's still young...
I'm not that old either, Ebony.
I know some of you don't trust me and think that I am where I am thanks to luck alone. That's fine.
We listen to you because you know your stuff. A full belly doesn't lie. Only greedy fools...
Beep! Beep!
Everyone looks in the direction of the noise to see the Djall, who claims to be the Consultant's assistant, reaching out to the piece of iron with a strange box.
She almost has it.

Beep—Beep—
Beep!
Oh, crap, the timing...
What are you doing?! Get your hand off our Oriron!
*Sarkaz expletive*, almost let you sneak it out under my nose!
What's the deal, Consultant?
I've got the door. They're not getting out.
Your rules say the penalty for stealing in this room is a lost hand, Consultant. You pick, Djall. Left or right?
She's not stealing. I asked her to... measure the Oriron content in the rock.
Uh, that's right! It's so high, it made my detector go haywire!
Be careful. I didn't hire you to give me a heart attack.
S-Sorry.
I'm not sure I believe you, Consultant. You think we aren't watching you?
It's one thing to help the Head-Sellers; at least they're sticking their necks out. But working with the tower-dwellers? That's another beast.
Why would a Djall girl in a pretty dress like that work as your assistant?
These are old clothes from my mom and dad. I just cut them to fit me...
That has nothing to do with what we're talking about today. The more important thing here is how we turn this piece of iron into food, isn't it?
How about this, I'll get someone willing to go over the walls to bring some food and take away this ticking time bomb, and then you can take your time to figure out how to share the food.
I changed my mind. Food's gone once you eat it. I want blades, Oriron! Oriron, and one hand from this thief. Do we have a deal?
Can you extract the Oriron?
I won't ask about your connections, Beck. You want the piece of iron, you can have it, but you got to supply our share of the food. Do the Broken Horns have that much food?
Didn't the Consultant call this a time bomb? You want food from me when I'm helping you solve a problem?
It fell in our yard. Food or Oriron, the Veteran's Home takes the biggest share.
Hah! Those streets belong to Broken Horns! You don't even get leftovers!
We'll follow the Consultant's rules. Everyone here gets a share. The mercenaries get an additional share to repair their roof.
Why does HE get to set the rules? Why not me?
Don't think for a second that we're scared of the Broken Horns, Beck.
You'll be arguing for the next hundred years at this rate. If the Broken Horns want this iron so much, they can have it.
Consultant...?
Let me finish. Any damages resulting from mishandling by the Broken Horns will also be Beck's responsibility.
Hmph.

As for the rest of you, whether it's food or Oriron you want, I'll take care of it. Consider it an apology for the little commotion just now. You can come get your share once I've figured out the amount.
Not for free, of course. My pockets aren't that deep. Any firework pieces that fell on your turf, you bring to me, no matter how big or small.

Don't be so short-sighted. The Liches may not be roaming the streets day after day now, but they're always watching us. Make sure you understand what you're allowed to take, and what you shouldn't even go near.
No objection. I picked up a lot of small pieces, but they're too small to be anything useful.
How does this work? How much food and Oriron can we get for the pieces?
Excuse me, I have an idea... the amount of Oriron can be calculated from the purity, but I'll need to take another measurement, since I got interrupted.
It would be best if I could take a small sample.
Pohl looks at Nymph with bewilderment in his eyes, but nods his head. The others give way for Nymph to take her peculiar hand-box to the cracked 'piece of iron'.
She takes out a strange rod and knocks on the surface lightly. A small, dull-colored piece falls out from inside.
Nymph's box opens slowly, sucking the piece in. When the box closes, everyone in the room feels compelled to look to the window.
The skies are still dark, but the light shining in seems to have gotten brighter and warmer.
Whew... done.
Write your choice of supplies and your name on the paper, and come back in three days. You know where to find me.
Doesn't matter if you can't write, Beck. You got the whole piece of iron; you're not getting anything else from me.
And I'll deal with the territorial dispute between the mercenaries and the Broken Horns, same day, next month. All of you better show up.
Here's the paper, just like you wanted, Consultant.
Anything else? Alright, you can get back to your own lives now.
Thank you so much, Consultant. You're really a huge help.
Ah, it's nothing. I've crossed paths with that Lich lady before, after all. It's the least I could do.
And please... call me Pohl.
I didn't expect the legendary Consultant to be so... young.
But the supplies that you promised the people here, to get me out of that jam—do you really have so much food and Oriron in stock?
Maybe? I've stocked up a bit over the years. Not sure if it's enough, though... maybe I should get a head start on picking up the firework pieces...
The clothes on your back are all wet.
Really? Well, I guess it's a hot day today.
Pohl, were you... scared?
Well...
(Of course I was scared!)
(Did you see how many men they had? And how many blades? They could have eaten me for dinner if they meant it!)
(What was I supposed to do? I just wanted to get everybody together and make our lives a little better. How was I supposed to know that things would turn out like this?)
I didn't sign up to become the Consultant!
Calm down, calm down! We're safe.
...You heard that?
What?
Djaj can read minds, can't they? Did you...?
It's okay, I didn't peek! Why does everyone think we can do that?
It's true that I'm a little more sensitive to people's fear... but it's very demanding Arts! And do you think I really want to hear what other people are thinking?
I guess you're right... it's not exactly pleasant stuff you would see in Kazdel.
Anyway, did you get what you needed?
Yeah. It's perfect. Look!
It's so small. But it does look unique.
It was surrounded by Oriron. That's why the detector didn't react.
Well, you got what you came for, didn't you?
No, I'm just getting started.
Ermi... my Lich friend says there's at least four or five more pieces in the city, and I have no idea where to find them. I was planning on asking someone well-connected...
But who's better connected in these parts than you? You must know something, right Consultant?
I have a rough idea where some of the pieces might have fallen, but I can't be sure whether they'll have revenants in them or not.
It's a start! Better than stumbling around the city trying my luck!
If you help me find the remaining pieces, I'll ask the lazy Liches to reimburse the food and Oriron you're handing out!
Well, food might be a little difficult, but Oriron shouldn't be a problem. There's always parts coming off the Furnace during maintenance!

Well, I guess I couldn't do worse...
Hey, stop tugging on my sleeve!
I'll take that as a yes! A little stroll would do you some good, given how stressed you are.
Who said I was stressed?
Did you forget I'm a Djall?
You said you didn't look!
My stuff is different from theirs! I picked everything nice and careful. Look at the quality! How could you give me the same price?
No difference. Three.
How about this, I got another handful of pure Oriron in my pocket. No Originium mixed in at all. I'll add it to the pile for a piece of your bread.
No.
Who do you think you are, old man? Three potatoes for three full baskets of Oriron scrap? Did you burn your conscience in the furnace too?
My bread is pure white flour. You're not getting it for a handful of slag. Get lost. Next!
Two baskets? Put it on the scale. Mm. Two sweet potatoes. Grab it from the ash. Don't try to swipe any; I guarantee you I'll know.
I told you to get lost, Bean Sprout. You're in the way.
You're not the only furnace keeper in this city, old man. If you won't take this, someone else will.
You can have this entire batch of bread if you get more than three sweet potatoes from anybody else, you dumb slug.
Everybody heard that? No takebacks!
*Sarkaz expletive*, they just keep getting worse! The furnace keeper this side's only giving two, and it's cellar leftovers from last year! How am I supposed to make a living?
My back's hurting from gathering this stuff all night. It's not even worth a day of food? Anyway, I'm not going back to the old man. Maybe I should check out the market on the east side.
But the path east would take me past those shacks...
The old folks say they dumped broken bodies there during the war, and there's still ghosts who couldn't get back to the myriad souls...
No, no, no! Only kids are scared of ghost stories. Of course I'm not scared.
Just a dark alley and a few abandoned shacks.
He makes up his mind and pushes his rickety cart forward, around scattered piles of firewood and puddles, toward the narrow and winding alley.
A dark borderline cuts open the path ahead. In front is the area in the shadow of the Soul Furnace. The gaunt man slows his steps, and covers the cart with his jacket.
(Gulps) It's just around the corner...
How is it so cold at midday? Hah, what's with the weather these days?!
Did something move? Who's there? Who said something?!
This place has been abandoned for ages... what's that sound? A blade being sharpened? Meat being cut?
*Sarkaz expletive*, I'll just run through with my eyes closed...
The cart crashes into a wall, just as he is about to get out of the shadow of the Furnace.
No, not a wall... his eyes run across grey-white metal as he looks up, until they meet a pair of crimson-red counterparts.
Ghost!
Sorry, I think I'm lost. Are you alright?
Are you blind? Didn't you see my cart? You, you...?
Sorry. My fault. See ya.
No, just... just stay there! Don't move!
What's up with the weather? How is it so hot when I don't even see the sun? I'm soaked with sweat.
So many weirdos in town these days...
You again? I thought you went west.
A pair of blood-red eyes rise slowly from the shadows in a corner, rising above the Sarkaz's head, above the shacks by the wayside, above the wilted trees.
The heavy figure turns slowly, emerging from the furnace's shadows. The tattered linen cannot hide the grey-white cracks, but they fill the Sarkaz's vision.
(Hoarse growl)
It-It's true... I... *Sarkaz expletive*.
The young Sarkaz's eyes roll into his head. He lets go of his cart and falls unconscious to the ground.
Let's take a break. There's water over there to wash your face.
Well, I got dirt all over my face, but at least we have something to show for it. If nothing else, we got that huge rock out of the construction site.
Who would thought that only a thin outer layer is iron, and the rest is just ash?
Where do we go next?
To the east side is the market. You can find all sorts of stuff there, but they close early, and it's practically a ghost town by the time the Furnace's shadow rises above the east wall.
Don't step on my writing practice, Nymph!
Oh, sorry, Cherie. Does your grandpa have you watching the fire by yourself again?
He said he was going to work at the construction site. He always comes back stinky and falls right asleep. Doesn't even tell me any stories.
Hey, Nymph, since you're here, how are these words?
You're practicing writing on the ash? But it's...
Fresh furnace ash is very clean!
Great work, Cherie! You got all the words I taught you last time right. I should give you a treat...
I've got a candy here.
Candy! But... I don't want candy. I'm afraid that...
Afraid of what?
That the monster in the alley might take it...
Over there, that haunted alley. They say ghosts come for you if you get close to the shacks.
They follow you and snatch your stuff when you're not looking!
You must be joking.
It's real! A lot of people have run into them. Some lost their candy, others lost the linens they put out to dry. They say the ghosts don't target you if you don't carry anything good.
They probably got robbed by some thugs and were too embarrassed to tell the truth.
Don't play with this guy, Nymph! He's bad, he won't believe me.
Alright, don't cry. You say a lot of people saw the, er, ghost?
Yeah! Ask Bean Sprout if you don't believe me. He just ran into one.
Yes, it was three stories tall, wrapped in bandages, and bleeding from its eyes.
Wait, didn't you say there were flames coming out from all over? Don't the bandages catch fire?
Yeah, ghostfire. It's cold, so it doesn't burn.
Er, alright, go on.
What else is there to say? What else could I do?
I did a backflip in midair and dodged the ghostfire, then aimed for its eyes with a handful of iron scrap. The monster never saw it coming.
A backflip? Just yesterday you said your back was hurting and asked me to carry your firewood to your door...
I must be a natural. It felt like energy just kept flowing out from inside me.
*sigh* We should ask someone else.
Don't! No one else could tell it half as good as me!
They all fainted when they saw the thing and ran for their lives without looking back when they came to their senses. They even left the carts they borrowed at the scene. What would they know?
You're not talking about yourself, are you?
What if I am...? You got a problem with me? I'm not afraid of you!
We really don't have time for this. Quit babbling and spit it out.
Pohl––Consultant, he just ran into something real scary, don't be too hard on him.
Wha-Consultant? You're the Consultant? Oh, I'm so sorry! I wouldn't have skipped anything if I knew the Consultant was here to interrogate me himself.
I wouldn't call it an interrogation...
It's those haunted shacks on the east side, I'm sure you know them. I ran into it there. It was crouched in a corner at first, so I didn't notice, but then I took a closer look—
It was draped in linen with blood-red eyes, twice as tall as me, and it was grinding its teeth as it was walking. I was so scared I fainted right there.
I was still in one piece when I woke up, so I thought I was dreaming at first, but then I reached behind my head and felt the bump where I hit it. See, it's right here—

That's fine, I don't need to see it.
You think that's the monster we're looking for, Nymph?
(Shakes head) We'd better go and see if the detector responds. It's not far.
Nymph, Consultant, that's the whole truth, whatever you lost, it really wasn't me. Life's hard enough as it is.
Don't worry, we're not here to give you trouble.
But you should get your cart back, or else I can't promise you that Big Head won't give you trouble.
The young Sarkaz's smile freezes. He pats his own head in chagrin, only to hit the fresh bump. He grimaces in pain.
Can I ask one more favor, Nymph...?
Here's the shack he was talking about. It's been abandoned for ages, though.
Is your detector reacting?
No... are we looking in the right place?
More likely the kid lied.
Does she know there are Sarkaz ashes in the pile she's writing in?
I'd guess no. You protect her well.
...Is that 'protecting'?
Of course it is. Anyway, it would be pretty embarrassing if we got tricked by a kid.
But we're here, and if it really is the work of some thug—

—We might as well take care of him.
Come in, there's no...
Oh.
Nymph is about to speak when she sees a blade pressing against the back of Pohl's neck.
The blade is blunt and chipped, but the hand is surprisingly steady, pressing against Pohl's neck three centimeters beneath his ear, without the slightest movement.
Nymph, don't.
The Djall has drawn her staff. The Arts wave emanating from it does not dissipate at Pohl's words and continues to intensify.
He won't kill me.
You wanna bet?
Put the blade down!
It's alright, trust me. No need for bets here. After all—
Argh—
I can handle one wounded warrior on my own.
Your hand is bleeding... but it's not Sarkaz blood. Who are you?
This is my home. You don't get to ask who I am.

No, this is Kazdel, home of the Sarkaz.
Let me see your face...
Haven't I seen you before? In the settlement outside the city. You were smuggling out food with... Mudrock was the name, I think.
Mudrock? I think I've heard... mentioned the name before. Are you with Rhodes Island?
If you know our boss, can you help me up? I can barely move my arm.
This is the third time I've said it. They're not smugglers. They went to work for food at the construction site.
Why didn't I go? I would have, if not for this arm.
If you're not smugglers, why did you come into the city while hiding in a haunted shack?
Where else can I stay? This is the only place where no one bangs down the door at night.
You can't sleep at night in the crowded areas, and you can't even go out during the day without someone tearing the door down for firewood.
There was a Caprinae thief who cut his horns short, stealing steel and bricks from the construction site... is he with you?
Definitely not. Why would I do something so stupid? Cutting my horns to pretend to be Sarkaz?
Not much better living in a haunted house and pretending to be a ghost.
We never pretended to be ghosts. Don't blame us when stupid people fool themselves.
Your friends weren't at the construction site to steal bricks and steel?
What a joke. I'm not even Sarkaz and I know that's impossible—
Are you seriously suspecting a Child of Soil and Stone of stealing bricks and tiles?
Child of Soil and Stone? That Mudrock's a Gargoyle? I never noticed...
It would make sense. Last time I saw Mudrock, she was working on houses outside the city, helping Infected from elsewhere start new farms.
Then again, why do you have to come into the city? Is there something you have to be here for, when you have a Child of Soil and Stone helping you?
Before we talk about the fact that it was Mudrock's decision to come into the city to begin with, what gives you the right to question us?
You think we're afraid of the Sarkaz in the city?
No... we're afraid of you.
(Sigh) When is your boss coming back? We can't wait all day.
She would normally be back by now, but without me, and with the colossi gone, it might take another hour or so.
Let's wait. I need to see their boss. They can't stay here in the city... it's too dangerous.
Ahem. You speak Sarkaz very well, sir. Is that a Leithanian accent? What's your name?
Save it.
Alright, Mr. Save It, would you please move inside a little? I would like to sit down too.
The sun sets little by little. Nymph looks at the detector in her hand; it is unclear what she is thinking.
Pohl stands up and sits down again, then stands up again, finally ducking down into a crouch, counting the gravel on the ground.
The shadow of the Furnace passes over the east wall. Blood from the wound seeps through the new bandages and coagulates.
The Infected with the injured arm stands up, draws the knife that he left standing on the ground, and peers at the shadow outside.
I'm going to look for them.
You coming?
Do you know which construction site they went to?
No. I'll check all of them if I have to.
You're just going to march through the streets like that?
A wounded outsider... how about this, I'll go with you, and if anyone asks...
Shh.
Pohl is still staring at the gravel.
He has separated it into four piles, sorted by color and size, which now tremble and collapse in a unique rhythm.
They're back.
Mudrock... they found it...
Thump. Thump.
Thump.
The heavy footsteps stop at the entrance to the alley. Nymph and Pohl soon hear the faint sounds of metal scraping—the sound of blades being drawn.
Mudrock—
I'm alright!
The Child of Soil and Stone walks out from the corner, seemingly oblivious to her friend's call and making no motion in particular, but the Infected who follow her put away their weapons.
Mudrock walks into the battered shack and throws her shovel in a corner.
Come in.
Sit.
Rope.
The three statements flow from Mudrock's mouth in the same tired tone, with virtually no interruption.
Nymph sits down on the stool opposite Mudrock. The Infected stream into the room, leaving behind two holding a rope that stretches into the alley.
A Djall?
Er, hello, Mudrock! Yes, I'm a Djall. My name's Nymph. My mom and dad are both Djaj. I don't know if you've seen my sister at Rhodes Island...
They are here?
Well, no. I came to...
She's trying to chase us out.
Don't interrupt, Stick. Go on.
We came looking for something. Someone ran into a terrifying monster near this shack, and we suspect it has something to do with what we're looking for.
My guess is that it's just a misunderstanding. There's no monster at all...
I know what you mean. We're the 'monster'. That's how the Sarkaz here see us.
No, that's not what I mean.
The Sarkaz here aren't much different from those in the settlement outside. They're here because they don't have anywhere else to go.
As far as monsters go, Sarkaz have been treated as monsters by other races for as long as Kazdel has stood.
You must know what I mean, Mudrock. Please, explain to them...
Mm. Probably not a misunderstanding.
What?
The monster, did it look like that?
Hey, a hand?
Who? Me? Er, okay.
(Anxious roar)
Wh-What the heck is that?
Hold it tight! Drag it in!
One, two! One, two!
With the rope tied around their waists, the Infected wrap it around their shoulders and pull with all their strength.
Pohl looks with astonishment at the thing being pulled out from the shadows.
It is about twice as tall as a man, its eyes blood-red, draped in tattered white linen, with metal wires and ropes tied tightly around it.
The monster.
Tie it up!
What? How? Where? You sure about this? That thing could collapse this rickety shack with a single kick.
Just listen to Mudrock. Tie it up.
How did you catch it, whatever this... THING is?
The detector is vibrating! Is that your colossus, Mudrock?
Yeah, I saw it by the road on the way back.
It was working just fine yesterday, maybe a little slow, but nothing was broken. Then it suddenly ran out in the middle of the night and wouldn't come back.
Couldn't sense it with Arts, either. It's like something was interfering... do you know what's going on?
Maybe it's under the influence of a revenant fragment.
Revenant? What do they have to do with it?
Well... actually, can I see the colossus first?
Be careful. It lashes out at anyone. All my guys here have taken a few hits.
Did it break, uh, Stick's arm?
No, he broke it in a fight over some work at the construction site.
Come on, Mudrock, you wouldn't stand up for me back there, and now you're doing this in front of some outsiders?
Did I not stand up for you? I took all his work. He spent the whole day in the sun without getting a single grain of corn.
Anything else I should know about the colossus?
It seems to be scared of me. It runs away as soon as it sees me. I would have caught it a long time ago, if I wasn't worried that it would topple the furnace if it got into the plaza.
Nothing else, really, Nymph... it's Nymph, right? Be really careful, if it was the revenant that made it go berserk.
There aren't many Djaj in Kazdel. I don't want to get one hurt, as soon as I met her.
A-Alright, thanks for the advice.
Nymph takes out her detector and cautiously approaches the colossus.
One step, two steps... as she takes the third step, the detector emits a screeching alarm.
So that's it!
No, it's not... it's there.
The Djall points to a crack on the colossus. A piece of rubble seems to have gotten lodged in it.
It is fixed to the colossus like a hateful eye, no matter how the colossus struggles.
I sense something strange.
It's pain that has lasted centuries... hatred that has lasted a hundred times longer.
(Hoarse howl)
This is a revenant fragment, Mudrock. I think that's why it went berserk.
People were attacked, but not injured. I didn't think about why that was, but now... I think it just doesn't hurt the Sarkaz.
...I see. The revenant are like Kazdel. They reject anyone who isn't Sarkaz.
We need to get the fragment out. Can you help me, Mudrock? You created this colossus, you should be able to control it.
Make it stay still. Just a little while will do.
Okay.
Mudrock raises a hand to the colossus. It seems to feel the threat and struggles even harder.
The metal wires began to break where they have been weakened by rust, stirring up clouds of dust as they hit the ground.
The red glow in the colossus's eyes grows brighter, and all present hear a cold, eerie voice in their heads.
Oppression... revenge!
Banish! Banish! Banish!
All you see is hatred... you could not even tell Sarkaz from outsider. You still raise your fists?
That is not what a revenant should be. Alright then... let it go.
Are you out of your mind?
...Got it. Scatter on the count of three. Three, two, one!
Banish!
Kazdel, for Sarkaz, only!
What are you, then?
You're not a revenant, or a Gargoyle. You're just a broken Arts Creation... shouldn't you get out of Kazdel too?
Mudrock, look out!
The colossus begins to emit smoke from every crack and fissure. It seems to grow larger, raising a right fist that has almost grown to the size of the shack.
It is about to bring it down on the young Sarkaz.
The Djall's staff starts to glow. She looks into the colossus's eyes and resolutely recites a few words in Sarkaz. The red light in the construct's eyes grows dimmer with each word.
The shadows behind Nymph become increasingly sinister, as though ready to leap from the gravel at any moment.
An instant before the shadow pierces the ground, Mudrock grabs her hand and snaps her fingers.
Snap—
The Arts that hold the colossus together are dispelled, and the construct collapses into a pile of gravel and stone. The fist that it tried to bring down turns into a strong gust of wind.
Before the dust settles, the faint, yellow sunlight has wiped away the last vestige of the sinister shadow.
I can find my way from here.
It's okay, I'm going to Rhodes Island's branch office here to report on the revenant anyway. We're going the same way.
Is it safe to put it in that little box?
Totally safe! It's Lichcraft.
As for you and your friends... I know the guard on duty today. I can have him sneak you out of the city without drawing attention.
Why should we leave?
Just like that colossus, there are a lot of people here who won't tolerate an outsider living next door.
Your people need to eat and sleep, but that's not any easier to come by in the city.
Maybe I didn't bring them here just to eat and sleep.
In the past, not so long ago, there were more than just Sarkaz in this city.
People came from far away to teach, heal, build. They weren't here just to eat and sleep.
You mean Babel... but they left, and you know as well as I do why they left.
It was just a dream, like the kind you get when you're running a fever. I'm glad it was part of my childhood, but it wasn't always a good thing for Kazdel and the Sarkaz.
Kazdel is fragile... it can't handle anything like that happening again.
Are you speaking for yourself, or for all Sarkaz? Kazdel is not so fragile, from my point of view, and the Sarkaz have never been that fragile.
The bad can be avoided. The good will always be good.
There are things that Kazdel has to face. Kazdel is the last hope for some people, and they can't live in the settlement outside the city forever.
You're Sarkaz. Your friends aren't.
They trust me, like I trust Amiya.
Amiya... Rhodes Island. I've heard about your ideals. They're... attractive.
Now I'm even more curious. What is Rhodes Island like?
You can come with me to see Amiya. It's not far, just past that street.
She's in Kazdel?
Let me do this first, though. I said I would, and the one I promised... I'd rather not get on their bad side.
Nymph waves the box. The spinning rocks within make no sound.
Mudrock nods and walks toward the Furnace.
(Yawn) You done chatting?
Yeah. Where do we go next?

I would say the market on the east side, but they're long done for the day.
Why don't we call it quits too?
Hm... but I feel like I forgot something. What could it be?
Oh no! Bean Sprout's cart!
Isn't Nymph back yet?
I'm gonna get a bump bigger than Big Head's head if he comes back first!
What are you worried for? Nymph always comes through.
Look, over there—Nymph!
My cart! Thank goodness!
Isn't there someone to thank besides goodness?
Thank you! Thank you so much! The ghost didn't catch you, did it?
Stop scaring Cherie with that ghost and monster talk! Just some thugs up to no good. The Consultant taught them a lesson.
But the homes there are in disrepair, and the roads are really narrow. You should avoid the place.
Really? But they saw a bunch of people getting chased around by the monster from one end of the city wall to the other!
Maybe the ghost is after you, Nymph.
Maybe you should teach me a few more words today, just in case you can't come tomorrow.
...I'll be fine, don't worry.
She's got a special way of showing concern.
It's getting late. I should head back before the Broken Horns start getting ideas without me around.
Meeting that Mudrock gave me an idea. Do you think some of the fireworks might have fallen outside the city?
How about this, I'll touch base with smugglers outside tomorrow, and take a look around the Military Commission garrison while I'm at it.
Alright, I'll check out the east market. If you find anything, call me immediately. Don't put yourself in danger.
No problem. See you later!
*cough* *cough*
Hey, Big Head! What happened to your hand? It's all swollen.
Oh, er, big guy, here's your cart.
Just leave it over there.
Well, er, if there's nothing else, I'll be going.
Get lost, I'm too tired to deal with your crap right now.
Whew, I thought you were going to punch me. Did you not eat at all today, Big Head? Why the sullen look?
What did you just call me?
Why is the front of the cart dented? And the handle cracked?
Come here! Let me hear you loud and clear.
Please, no, help, mercy!
What is it, Ermi?
How's your progress after two days?
I've found lots of them! ...Well, okay, just two, actually... But don't you worry, I'll have it all wrapped up in no time! Why, what's the matter?
Nothing, really. The old man suddenly remembered this little mission of yours, so he's been pestering me about it.
We thought these fragments would be causing chaos all over Kazdel, but for the time being, things still seem normal as ever.
Nobody's knocking on the Military Commission's door about it, and we haven't detected any abnormalities either.
(*whispers*) All thanks to me being the first to run into them, haha...
Basically, feel free to keep going at your own pace.
Is that all?
Yep, that's all. Just remember, safety first. If you run into anything you can't handle, give me a call.
Sure, will do. Thanks, Ermi.
Ermi's thoughtful as ever, even while she works. I should probably pick up the pace.
So, whaddya want to eat?
Eh? Oh, sorry, let me have a look.
Uhh...
I'll try this one.
Is this a new recipe?
Just baked dough, same as always. Pretty sure I've seen you eat here before, too.
But the taste is different, no?
It's chewier than last time. Maybe even a little bit... sweet?
Really? Maybe my ingredients are better this time around.
More and more folks have been coming back lately, and I got ways to get quality stuff at a lower price. Dough gets nicer, that's a bonus.
Then, do you still need my help negotiating prices?
Nah, an old merc customer of mine put me in touch with a better supplier. They've got reasonable prices, and they're a friendly bunch. No need to trouble yourself any longer.
Happy to hear that! Does this mean one day, you could make white bread like they do in Victoria?
You little glutton, what's so great about white bread? Next time you've got time, I'll cook you up some grilled meat.
Really?! Mmm, grilled meat...
Can I have three more buns of the dough to go? I'll eat them on the road.
Sure. Just wait a sec and I'll give you some fresh out the oven.
Hehe, thank you.
Hmm-hmm. I've gotten so much done over the last two days, I think I deserve a little treat for myself.
Nothing beats a full belly—
Hmm?
Nymph glimpses a Sarkaz out the corner of her eye. Not on purpose, of course.
Any passerby would find themselves turning their head. It would be hard not to.
She stands motionless, leaning against her cane, with her top-hatted head held high.
Here, where most are disheveled and scruffy-looking, a woman like her is a rare sight indeed.
Here, your dough.
Eh? Oh, thanks...
First time laying eyes on her?
I don't think I've ever seen anyone quite like... her.
She wasn't always this way. Not sure when she went mad. Left Kazdel to make a name for herself, came back looking like this.
Oh... Well, even so, she does look pretty nice.
What's the point of looking nice? Can't sell a thing if you just stand there all day.
She sells baked dough too?
As if. She makes jewelry out of garbage.
Some odd gewgaws too, like a transparent glass witchcraft orb with a model of Kazdel inside, or cups etched with strange patterns. It's all expensive, fragile stuff. Only a fool would buy any of it.
Heard she's got ornaments and statues on top of all that. Even less business on those. Can't get any use out of them most days, and so expensive too. Don't know what she's thinking.
Everyone around has been telling her, but she won't listen to a word of it.
Ahaha...
I don't think it's a bad thing, getting to see someone dressed up so pretty on the street every day.
I just hope she earns enough to feed herself... It'd be some terrible joke if she starved to death in front of my stall, right?
Hmm, I see where you're coming from... I'd better get going, see you.
Be seeing you, Nymph. Bring some friends next time.
I will! Bye-bye!
*phew*... Now that I'm full, I should get back to work.
(She's so pretty...)
(Is that how they dress in Victoria? Those fabrics look really expensive.)
(And there's that brooch of hers with those purple and red gems...)
(Once I'm done with this job and I get my reward from Ermi, I'll go have a look at her stall!)
Wait, there's one here?
No way, there was no response just a moment ago.
Where is it, then?
Wait...
That brooch with the purple and red gems...?
Nymph feels a sudden tickle up her spine. She turns to look at the stall devoid of customers.
But no one is there. The behatted stall owner has long since packed up her wares and disappeared into the endless stream of people flowing through Kazdel.
Ermi, please pick up, it's urgent.
What now...?
If she's only wearing all that jewelry because she's possessed, then she...
There's no time. I'll go looking for her first.
Hello, do you know where the lady who runs this stall went off to? The one wearing a suit and top hat—
