Huh? A suit of what? Uh... She's wearing really strange clothes, and selling stuff no one wants to buy!
She went that way? Okay, thank y—The baked dough?
I got it from the stall over on the corner. Here, have some. If you like it, make sure you go to that stall!
I'm almost out of the city at this point. What did she come here for?
What's the detector say...? A very weak reaction, but it's here, alright.
I'll just have to check over everything.
It's here.
A warehouse?
She lives here?
No way, why would someone wearing such nice clothes live here?
Maybe it's because of... the revenant fragment?!
I can't reach Ermi, and Pohl's looking for fragments somewhere else.
C'mon, Nymph, think, think! There's got to be a way!
Hypnosis, mental suggestion, Originium Arts... Maybe I could snatch it away?!
Hello, young lady. Did you need me for something?
Sorry, let me think a bit longer...
Very good, ma'am.
Ah.
H-Hello?
Crownie Mantel, Elegance Extraordinaire. A pleasure to meet you. Now, how may I be of service?
Could you sell me that brooch?
Why?
Because, because...
Because I really like it!
Oh?
Well, I'm terribly sorry, ma'am, but it is not for sale.
Moreover, I'm afraid it's not suited to your ensemble.
I have here an adornment much more appropriate for a rose such as yourself. If your need is dire, I could give it to you.
Sorry, I'm only interested in the brooch.
Nymph looks into Crownie's eyes. Her arms hang by her sides as her fingers sway lightly at an unusual frequency, tracing complex patterns in the air.
They are reflected in Crownie's pupils, but as she blinks, they are gently wiped away. It seems they have not dug deep into her heart as Nymph expected.
I'm afraid that's not possible.
I will not part with this gem before finding a suitable replacement.
Now, I have some pressing business to attend to, so I'm afraid I cannot entertain you any longer. On your way now.
(Waves staff)
(Chants softly)
Please wait a moment, and hear my plea.
Nymph projects her Arts with all her will, and they descend gently upon Crownie.
One second, two...
Her target continues to walk away.
...No effect?
She's wearing the fragment. Getting around the revenant's influence on her sure isn't easy.
This is a really stupid way to do it, but...
I'm sorry!
Forgive me, Madam Djall, but I'm in a hurry. Please don't block my way.
Ermi, please, please, please answer.
Ermi!
Arts don't work, I can't fight her, and I can't snatch it away.
She's no normal hawker!
Just follow her for now, Nymph. Let's see where our ancestor wants to take her.
Even if something happens, I can at least give more info to Ermi.
It's not like she's going to go all the way out of the city, right?
We're on the outskirts already. Where's she going?
Maybe she really is leaving the city?
I should contact the Liches at the gates, then. Ermi gave me their info. They should be able to get the fragment for me.
Eh? Where'd she go?
Wait, what?!
Nymph watches as Crownie opens her own box, drawing out a hang glider from the depths within.
One hand grips the cane hooked onto the glider's frame, while the other holds the box tight. With her characteristic ramrod-straight figure dangling from the glider, she swoops off into the distance.
Can a revenant fragment really do all that?
I guess I should...
Forget it, Ermi's being awfully unreliable right now.
Wait, how about I...?
Nymph hems and haws for a while as she watches the glider float away, but in the end, she makes up her mind.
She inserts the tip of her key into the keyhole on her staff. It breaks apart, turning into a swarm of keys surrounding her.
She grabs one and opens the bag at her waist, the keys immediately splitting into two groups as she does so.
One flock of keys attaches to her staff, rapidly spinning like a drone's rotor wing.
The others act as platforms under Nymph's feet, holding her aloft despite gravity's pull.
Standing in midair is an extremely strange experience.
Drifting off wherever the wind blows, watching the familiar sights behind slowly shrink away.
Until the jagged edges of Kazdel have faded into fuzziness under the gentle light of the sun.
Then, with the breeze in back and the wind ahead—
A moment's distraction means tumbling to the ground.
AHHHHHH!
Hah... Hah...
Here. Use my handkerchief to wipe your sweat.
Ah... Th-Thanks...
I must admit, I have never seen anyone act quite so recklessly. You are inexperienced with those Arts, are you not?
Y... Yes...
If you hadn't flown by to catch me, I'd be a big splat on the ground.
Thank you for the hanky.
Oh, don't mention it.
Do you always use your glider to leave the city?
It's quick, convenient and graceful. A most beautiful method of transportation. I was inspired by the cable cars plying Kjerag's snowy peaks.
You've been to Kjerag?
I will have been one day, but for now, the pictures in the newspaper will suffice.
You should head back home already, young Djall.
What a strange ancestor... Why's he in such a hurry to leave Kazdel? And why bring Crownie along?
Oh, there's no signal here...
Guess you're on your own, Nymph. Your big adventure begins here.
Oh thank goodness, she finally stopped.
It looks like she's not leaving Kazdel just yet.
In the distance, Crownie takes a deep breath, then yells a strange... incantation?
'Come on out, Suffering!'
'Suffering'? Is that some kind of incantation?
It might be a name of a Sarkaz King. It wouldn't be strange for an ancient King to have a name like that.
If you insist, old buddy.
Heheh, long time no see.
A-A Sankta?!
His plan involves a Sankta?!
What the heck is he up to?!
I can see you, little Djall.
No need to hide behind a rock, come on out.
Why's your apprentice being such a stranger?
She's not. I believe there might be some misunderstandings at play here. She has been following me for quite some time now.
If you keep wandering around with that brooch on, I think your face'll end up on an arrest warrant real quick.
I'll take it under advisement, Mostima.
May I see the goods I ordered?
But of course.
Mostima gingerly places the box she is carrying between the two of them.
Three bolts of top-quality soft silk straight from Yan, just like you asked. Go ahead, inspect all you like.
This... This is...
I can feel it...
This subtle push-back when you press down on it, this suppleness, this pure white...
I guess he really does want to leave Kazdel. He's even got a bunch of supplies ready.
Huh? Where'd that Sankta go?
Heya, little Djall. Nice weather today, isn't it?
S-Stay back! I-I'm a caster! I've got really really super strong Arts!
Ooh, what a coincidence. As it turns out, I'm a caster too. Makes us counterparts, don't you think?
But you're... I'm...
It's fine, it's fine. You look reasonable enough, not the type to attack a defenseless Messenger like me for no reason.
I'll take your silence as a good sign.
Crownie's gonna need a while longer to check out her haul, so I'll get some fresh air over here, maybe slack off a bit. Won't bother you at all. How's that sound?
So, you're a Messenger?
Penguin Logistics. Guaranteed success.
Wanna try some Lateran candy?
Thanks... Then, you can have some of this.
What is it?
Baked dough from Kazdel. Shame it's gone cold... It's really tasty when it's hot.
Well, don't mind if I do.
So... how do you know Crownie?
I probably shouldn't tell you. Client confidentiality and all. But... You sure you wanna listen to a Sankta story?
Who knows, I might weave a web of lies to snare your soul.
Go ahead. I can tell truth from lies.
They say the Sankta are all awful and terrible, but... You haven't shot me with a gun yet, and you haven't tried to cut off my horns for a trophy on your belt.
Haha, alright. I'll start talking, then.
Hmm... Where to begin?
A few years ago, on a job in Ursus, I ran into a Sarkaz.
Apparently, she couldn't stand life at home and decided to wander across the lands. By the time I met her, she had just managed to find a path she wanted to follow.
Ursus has real strict controls on a lot of things, so that Sarkaz needed people like us to get her the stuff she wanted.
Was Crownie a designer in Ursus?
A designer? Heh, an Ursine hiring a Sarkaz to be an assistant is already a super-nice Ursine.
Y'know, the fashion brand that hired her was actually founded by a Sarkaz, but it got so crazy-popular in Ursus most people think it's local.
Even that fashion freak over there thought so at first. Anyway, she got the cold shoulder in Ursus, so after a bunch more wandering, she made her way back here.
She must have made a lot of money before she came back.
Money? Hehe, shame I have to say it, but she's barely got two coins to rub together.
When you look at her, well, you're seeing everything she's got, in a way.
The silk's perfect, Mostima!
Then why's she buying cloth? I've seen where she lives, and the people around her. Maybe she's skipping meals to afford all this expensive stuff...?
Maybe she predicted that the Furnace would explode, and wanted the revenant to latch onto the fabric? No, that doesn't make any sense...
Wowee, that sounds serious.
I have no clue what you're talking about, but we should probably head on over. C'mon.
Need anything else, Crownie?
My thanks, Mostima. You must have been through a lot, coming all this way.
Nah, just doing my job.
As for further work, I would like some fabric from Sargon. I presume I will need to give full payment in advance, just as before?
I mean, sure, but I don't think you'll be needing my services much longer.
What ever do you mean? Is your company changing its modus operandi?
No, don't get the wrong idea here.
What I mean is—
This sort of stuff will be sold in Kazdel soon enough, so you won't need Penguin Logistics.
Is that so...?
The number of caravans going in and out of Kazdel has doubled several times over. The folks at Rhodes Island have also helped set up a few connections for the city.
You'll probably have a place you can get imported goods before long.
So you're saying... I will be able to sell my Kazdelian souvenirs?
Your what?
Crystal balls, cups... You know, souvenirs you'd get on a trip. Not that different to the ones you see in other countries, they're just Kazdel-themed.
But who would want to go on vacation to Kazdel? We don't really have any good sightseeing spots.
I wouldn't mind, to be honest. Walk around, see your Soul Furnace, roam the streets, and there's that 'Temple of Knowledge'... That's in Kazdel, right?
*sigh* Shame I won't get the chance. Your idea's not bad, though. You should talk to the merchants. They might want to buy some souvenirs to show off back home.
I'll bear it in mind. My thanks. As for you, young Djall...
Call me Nymph.
Let us go, Madam Nymph. We should hurry on home.
Okay. Well, see you, Sankta Messenger lady!
Ciao. If you ever need anything, remember to call Penguin Logistics. Crownie's got our contact.
Phew... Shouldn't you have popped out too, 'Suffering'? Or is it 'Mad Bomber' these days?
Have you lost your mind? Coming to Kazdel just for a delivery?
If you get caught by the Sarkaz, there's no way you're making it out alive.
She's an old customer. Not wise to refuse a request.
What, worried about me?
Supervising you is my job. We're going back right now. Immediately.
Alright, alright. Not like there's anywhere to run in this wasteland anyway. Say, why don't we go have some fun in Kazdel for a day?
(Raises weapon) ...Start walking.
*sigh* It's so hard to get through to you.
Welcome to my humble abode.
After an entire day of walking, Nymph and Crownie are back in front of the warehouse where they first spoke.
The place that Nymph assumed the revenant compelled Crownie to go to avoid the crowd.
(She really does live here.)
(There's a bed, a workbench, some clothes hung up, a corner full of junk. She's missing a lot of basic furniture.)
When you look at her, well, you're seeing everything she's got, in a way.
Please, make yourself at home.
Sure...
Do you need help, Crownie?
No. I can handle things on my own.
That cloth you bought is really expensive.
A mere several months' worth of food, hardly important.
Hmm, this place is ill-suited for a brooch. If I use this cloth to make a matching necktie or cravat...
Madam Nymph.
What is it?
You wanted this brooch, did you not?
Eh? Yeah.
But didn't you—
Take it. I no longer have need of it.
A heavy stillness emanates from the brooch, and Nymph cannot stop herself from shivering. She quickly throws the fragment-embedded jewelry into her box.
This whole time, the revenant fragment seems to have done nothing other than latch onto the brooch.
No commanding strange creations, no instigating arguments in a crowd.
When he was in the palm of her hand, she even felt some fury and frustration coming from him, like...
...Talking to a burdenbeast?
Pardon?
Oh, no, it's nothing, just a funny idea that popped into my head... Are you feeling alright, Crownie?
Thank you for your concern, but I've been fit as a fiddle this entire time.
(She doesn't sound any different from earlier. Has she really recovered from it?)
If there's anything I can do to help, don't hesitate to ask.
Hmm... As it were, Madam Nymph, I do have a presumptuous request.
Just Nymph is fine, no need to be so formal.
Very well then, Nymph. Could you come here for a moment?
Nymph looks at the clothes atop Crownie's workbench. They appear to be a suit, but not in Crownie's size.
Could you try this on?
Me?
Crownie whips out a tape measure, rapidly taking Nymph's measurements while muttering strings of numbers to herself.
It should fit you. Give it a try.
Aren't these clothes really expensive? I'm scared I'll—
Don't worry. It's not finished yet.
Alright, then.
H-How do I look?
Beautiful.
How is the fit? Are the cuffs too tight? Too loose? Anywhere that feels particularly awkward?
No, it's a perfect fit...
Are you making these clothes for a friend?
No. It is a gift to myself.
This outfit's creation is my dream, but alas, I am still unable to realize it.
I think it's pretty much perfect already.
Far from it, I'm afraid. The cuffs still need embellishment, the accessories still need matching. All in all, still rather crude...
You may take it off now, Nymph. My thanks.
Oh, okay.
But why would you make an outfit you can't wear?
Because its revelation gave my life direction.

I wanted to distinguish my life from those of others, but I too love this city. And so, I called attention to myself through a multitude of methods, old Sarkaz tricks all. I'm sure you'll understand where I'm coming from.
In the end, I was driven away from Kazdel, set adrift in the wilds.
I was offered menial work in Ursus. At first, it was only my survival instinct that spurred me to accept.
But I found myself rather suited to deft needlework. More importantly, I obtained a designer's manuscript.
A Sarkaz designer from Kazdel. Kazdel, designer. Never had I thought those two words could fit together.
Were these clothes in that manuscript?
They were. It was a heavily damaged book, and much information was lost, save for a note reading 'for AM' and the framework of this suit. Naught else remained.
Despite the damage, it provoked an emotion I had never felt before.
In order to understand it, I took up tailoring, alongside the crafting of many other knick-knacks.
As it happened, Kazdel had forgotten me by that point. And so I returned, under a new identity.
Kazdel has changed, just as I have. It's a more tolerant city these days. Even a stuck-out nail such as I can find a stage here.
Is that why you made stuff you can't sell?
They are souvenirs. There will come a day that Kazdel will entertain visitors from all across Terra. This, I do believe.
Though I am merely testing the waters on a whim, if other places can enjoy something, then why not Kazdel? We are not so different from them.
It could be a suit of fashionable clothes, a set of baubles for tourists, or even... a way of life. The pursuit of beauty.
You might be thinking too far ahead. I've never even dreamed of Kazdel being a tourist attraction!
You're right that the city has changed lately, though. There's lots of people walking around, and it's real lively these days. Completely different from before.
Did you see the fireworks a few nights ago? They were so pretty! Maybe the Furnace got a bit too happy and lively, and that's why it fired them off.
Nymph, what's going on?
Everything's fine now, Ermi. Ran into some trouble while I was trying to get the fragment, but they're all taken care of.
Good to hear. I was helping the old— *cough* aiding my master in some research on phylactery, so I didn't catch your calls. Terribly sorry about that.
It's fine, I've covered most of the city by now. There's just one newly-repaired residential district left to check. I might be finished by tomorrow, so you'd better get that reward ready!
Once you're done, come right over and it's all yours. You're a lifesaver.
I'll be turning in early tonight. Goodbye.
Sure, see you soon, Ermi.
Well, I won't trouble you any longer, Crownie. If anything comes up, remember to come find me. Here's my contact.
I will, thank you. Good night, Nymph.
Good night.
Hah... I'm so tired...
I never got to eat any of that baked dough I bought this morning.
*munch*
Hmm... I think if I warm it up once I get home, it'll taste a lot better.
Pohl! How did you find me?
You walked up to the city gates and flew off the walls. The information brokers've been chatting about you pretty much all day.
Did you find anything?
Nope. No signs of fragment influence around the Military Commission garrison or settlement.
You found something yourself, didn't you?
Yeah! Look! The fragment I found today is super pretty! It's like a gem!
It's a piece of glass from inside the furnace, looks like. You're right though, that color is really distinctive.
The lady who gave it to me was really distinctive too!
Today's been real hard on me, though. I was running around all day, and now I'm hungry and tired, haaah...
Go home and get some rest already. Just one residential district left to check. Gotta do all we can to get everything wrapped up tomorrow.
Alright...
Damn, you're so tired you nearly smacked your head against the wall... Fine, I'll walk you home.
Don't fall asleep just yet. Tell me about this 'distinctive lady' you met.
Sure... I went to the market this morning to buy...
Some baked dough, which smelled real nice...

eps.name: The Witch and Her Afternoon Tea

lines.x: Where am I?
Hey—
Hey—
How did I get here?
My hand...
Pink patterns have formed on her hand. Blood has frozen into crystal, tearing the skin apart, and forcing its way out along the patterns.
A chill wind blows past, tearing blood and flesh like flower petals, leaving behind only—
It hurts... why...
Half an hour ago...
I'm sorry, Nymph, I hate to rush you like this.
But I won't have peace here until these revenant fragments are back where they belong.
Master and Wiš'adel have been fighting constantly. They sound crazier than the ancestors in the furnace!
I'm sorry, Ermi... I've practically combed every inch of Kazdel, but I just couldn't find the remaining fragments.
Please, I'm counting on you. What about that slum kid who was with you?
He ran the number of households in this area against how fast we walked and figured that we had to split up to check everything before sundown.
We split it down the middle. He said he had some repairmen on his side with sticky fingers, and he wanted to take the opportunity to teach them a lesson.
I hope he's not putting himself in danger... but I'm sure he can handle it.
Hey, come on, it was just a few questions. Why the weapons?

You can't expect to run a dirty operation without facing the music. And I never said I was gonna report you to the Military Commission... Alright, if it's just the two of you, I could use the exercise.
What? Those guys are with you too? Er, how many are there? Twenty? Thirty?
Um, is it too late for negotiations?
Do you think a revenant would miss the Furnace after a few days and go home on his own?
Are you even listening to yourself?
Would you want to go back to the place where you were held prisoner for centuries?
It's just an idea... I don't know what it's like in the Furnace.
You know, maybe Kazdel is a kind of furnace, too. People outside think it's scary and terrible, but it's home to us.
It's hard to imagine being imprisoned for hundreds of years when I've been around for less than twenty. Maybe you're in a better position to judge, Ermi.
I wouldn't carry on down this topic if I were you, Nymph.
Heheh...
You really are something else, Nymph. Probably no one else in Kazdel who would think like that but you, our kindest Djall.
Huh? What'd I... (Sniffs) ...Do I smell herbal tea?
Oh, I remember now! Qalaiša's place is right over there. Should we pay her a visit?
Nymph! We have more urgent business, remember?
Right, uh, I was suggesting we go ask about the revenant fragments... who said anything about berry cookies? Are the fragments not urgent business?
Fine... But don't take too long.
It's a wonder you get along so well with Qalaiša, though.
She's wonderful! She always gives me little presents when I visit. Why is everyone scared of her?
Oh, but I can't go empty-handed, official business or not!
Let me see. She only moved in recently. What would be a good housewarming gift...?
Knock, knock. With one hand behind her back, Nymph knocks on the slightly damp door with the other.
Are you home, Qalaiša?
Oh, hello, Nymph.
Just a second while I get the door.
Good afternoon, Qalaiša!
I didn't have time to prepare a gift, but I hope you like this anyway.
I remember you said you wanted some flowers to cover up the smell of herbs in the house.
Oh, careful of the mud, I picked them with the roots so you can plant them in your garden.
A pair of hands, fair as ivory, takes the flowers. Blue petals fall upon her gloves like the patterns of fine embroidery.
Blue snowdrops... can Kazdel's barren soil nurture such beauties now?
Thank you, Nymph. I'll place this gift in the finest vase.
Glad you like it!
I didn't know they were called blue snowdrops.
I'm delighted that you came to visit, Nymph, but unfortunately, I'm about to head out.
Oh, you're busy? Is this a bad time? I'll—
Not at all. Just a small errand. It shouldn't take more than a few minutes. You can wait here.
Just don't touch anything while I'm out.
Some things are... not ready, or suitable, to be presented. Promise me?
I promise! I won't touch anything. My parents taught me manners.
Good girl.
Nymph sits down by the desk. The warm candlelight is like a fragile barrier against the shadows from the corners and the roof.
A faint, acidic scent wafts from the herbs hanging from the roof beams. The wooden cabinets by the wall stand like coffins, guarding secrets that must never see the light of day.
Whew, my heart's racing for some reason.
What's that creaking sound? Is there a musbeast in the walls?
Nymph gulps. She feels a chill run up her spine.
She keeps glancing at the window, but the daylight outside seems to grow darker every time she does.
Her heart races with an unusual rhythm. The Djall touches her chest, and feels a trembling—box.
Oh, it's just the detector.
Wait!
It wasn't reacting when I was outside. Could the fragment be in this house?
My back's starting to hurt from sitting too long. I should stretch my legs. It's alright if I just took a look around, isn't it?

This way? That way? Up there? It's not under the floor, is it?
There! The signal's getting stronger...
Ouch!
Where did this cabinet come from? Was it here all along?
Oh no, I knocked something off!
Qalaiša is gonna think I messed with her stuff! I've got to put it back...
Surely it's fine to put back something that fell on the floor?
Nymph bends down and feels for the blurry shadow in the darkness.
A strange sensation strikes her fingertips. She lifts the shadow, revealing a garland of branches, thorns, and mossflower, with snowdrops still mixed in.
(In Ancient Sarkaz) Snow?
Nymph feels her heart skip a beat upon saying the ancient Sarkaz word, like the chill she gets when an actual snowflake falls on her palm.
She stares at the garland in her hand, resisting the urge to put it on.
The room seems to shrink and darken, until it's almost suffocating. The sound of breathing in her ear grows heavier, but is it her own, or from... behind her?
The branches entwine with her hair, and the thorns pierce her palm. Nymph silently counts, then turns slowly but resolutely to look behind her—
Please take it.
Huh?
You—when did you... who are you?
Please take it...
Please take... it...
The walls of the hut fall and the roof is gone. I find myself in the wilderness, the chaos of battle coming from beyond the dust.
The garland in my hand has turned to blood, spilling through my fingertips.
A bloodied nachzehrer crawls toward me, but I cannot move.
Help... me... Master...
Who are you?!
Tell the Liege... the King of Sarkaz is dead. Yliš has fallen.
Why... do we still fight?
I hear the ancient Sarkaz language, disjointed syllables that come together to form words—
'King', 'Death', 'Fight'.
I try to ask him where we are, but my words slip away from my lips, turning to faint echoes in the sky.
Where am I?
The end of the battlefield... the boundary between life and death...
Why do you not grant me your blessing, Master? Do you despise my cowardice too?
Grant me the pain of decay, grant me the continuation of life... take this power away.
Please take it...
He pulls out a rotten object from his chest and places it in my hand.
His visage contorts from pain. His eyes are a mixture of reverence, fear... and hate.
G-get it away from me!
I don't want it! Help!
I understand... this is not a blessing, but punishment for my desertion.
Please... Master Qalaiša.
You called me... Qalaiša?
Wait, why is my hand acting on its own?
Is this a trick, Qalaiša? Stop it!
I raise my hand at the broken figure, but the hand I see is wrapped in bandages soaked with dried blood.
A tattered cloth brushes against the deserter's helmet. The scent of decay flows out, tearing away at what remains of his reason, filling his husk, bringing... life.
I feel a daze, then hunger. I bestowed strength on my weakened kin, becoming stronger in the process.
Without speaking, I hear a familiar voice in my head.
Cast down your weapon, deserter, and leave my battlefield.
As you wish, Master.
He casts down his heavy armor, piece by piece, revealing terrifying wounds through gaps in the cloth that shrouds him.
The blood seeping out of his body coagulates instantaneously, like new skin.
Have you seen snow before?
The sound of the word is peculiar, but I understand what it means.
It has been so long since it last snowed in Kazdel, many have forgotten how to pronounce the word.
I killed an Ursine and cleaved open his chest. I watched as he died, but he didn't seem to feel any pain, repeating only a single word over and over.
(Saying the Ursine word for snow)
It must be something that removes the pain. Have you seen snow, Master?
Pain is the shadow of strength, and life is the sun. I cannot see snow under the scorching sun.
You're afraid of pain, but not of death?
But why we live in... nay, it is not my place to say.
I should go, Master.
Go north, and don't come back.
The scene before me fades into the distance. I am rapidly moving away from the ground, and toward the battlefield.
I see helmets clash and swords strike against armor. I see the shadow of death looming over Kazdel.
Where am I?
Hey—
Hey—
How did I get here?
My hand...
Pink patterns have formed on my hand. Blood has frozen into crystal, tearing the skin apart, and forcing its way out along the patterns.
A chill wind blows past, tearing my blood and flesh like flower petals, leaving behind only—
It hurts... why...
Come.
Give me your hand.
Qalaiša?
She holds my hands tightly and recites an incantation that I have never heard before.
I see clear droplets of water hanging from her eyelashes. Perhaps it is melted snow.
Phew—
She exhales and lets my hands go.
The comforting warmth is still there. I spread my palms open—
The patterns and wounds are no longer there, replaced by a pair of blue butterfly wings, which flap and disappear into the snowfield.
Is this real?
Yes, it is. Come, I'll tell you on the way.
Qalaiša...
Have we met before? It's alright, you may call me the Northwind Witch here.
I think you're the first Nachzehrer I've seen since I came here.
Nachzehrer? I...
I've sensed your presence ever since you set foot on the snowfield. You are growing weaker, like a campfire buried under snow.
A Nachzehrer who turns from war inevitably grows weak, a process that is even more painful than waiting for the approach of death.
So, why did you do it?
She throws the question at me casually, like kicking up a puff of loose snow.
But the question feels so heavy I can only gasp for air. Words fail me.
Was it because of 'that' war?
The Liege, nay, Nezzsalem, had no choice. And you...
You had to take the opposite path, as I did.
We're here.
Behind a cracked boulder lies a small patch of ice where wind and snow cannot reach. Beneath this ice, the water remains calm, cradling a round air pocket like a sorrowful eye.
The Northwind Witch cracks the edges of the icy surface with her staff, and the air pocket escapes, becoming one with the wind and snow again.
I came across this place by chance. The musbeasts won't drink from it, even if they're dying of thirst.
This spring does not grow or diminish, its water always clear. It exists on its own, outside of this snowfield.
I once bandaged myself by this spring, but later discovered that immersing in it was far more effective.
Like, you bathe in it? I've heard that workers on the mobility layer bury barrels in the ground to bathe, but it's so cold here...
The water is not deep. You can hold my hand, if you're afraid.

Alright, I... I guess I'll try.
Eek!
I step slowly into the spring. The cold penetrates the armor and bandages that do not belong to me, piercing my every bone.
The water covers my shoulders and presses against my heart.
Qalaiša, no, Northwind Witch... do you remember encountering a dying Nachzehrer deserter, a long time ago?
I do.
Did he ever see the snow?
I heard he died on his way to the north.
But he saw the snow, at least, didn't he?
No. He may never have seen it, but the snow was always inside him.
Hm...
The heartbeat gradually slows and I can hear the sound of snowflakes falling on my head in between the rhythm.
The festering wounds and old scars continue to sting. The withered flesh and blood will never flourish again. But none of it matters anymore.
The pain has not lessened. It simply exists in another form now, like snowflakes that melt in the icy spring.
Qalaiša... is this really a dream?
If it is, who did you make it for?
What do you want to tell them? Your past, your future... or who you really are?
Qalaiša does not answer. She sits by the shore, her staff laid sideways on her legs.
I see a blue-purple butterfly fly past her hair.
One after another, they encircle the icy spring, turning the decay in me into new butterflies as they flap their wings.
Crystalline butterflies surround her. Her fingertips drop down to brush the surface of the water, but there is no ripple.
The snow stops and I suddenly realize that so too has this dream.
Is the dream over?
Qalaiša? Can I leave now?
You're scaring me. I could really use some hot tea right now...
...Qalaiša?
In the darkness, I sense something approaching, but whatever it is is not alive.
I try to curl up but cannot sense my limbs or torso. It is as if only my consciousness is left.
(Malicious sounds)
I feel... fangs. I sense... hunger.
I arrive... beyond the end, before the door.
Qalaiša, help! I shouldn't have touched your stuff, I'm sorry!
Qalaiša!
Cold engulfs me again and fear rushes into my throat. Just as I am certain I will drown, the door opens.
Come in.
You have rejected the title that the Military Commission conferred upon you, Qalaiša.
Do you know what you have turned down? A war that we have yearned for too long.
The blood of the old days will not be washed away. Don't tell me you really intend to leave with that house? The Liches will never allow you to leave with their handiwork...
Why is there a Djall here?
Er... hello... my liege?
Are you... King Nezzsalem? I read about you in my textbooks...
The tall Nachzehrer bends down toward me. I cannot tell whether he is observing, listening, or trying to discern my scent.
Why have you come? She has not polished this memory, nor connected it to other things.
I-I don't know. One minute I was in an ice spring with her and the next thing I know I'm here.
Um, what do you by not connected to other things?
Qalaiša deserted her war, but never her kind.
She preserved her memories... for her kin, for reasons unknown.
Perhaps she discovered a new path in the endless icefields...
Do you mean we're in Qalaiša's memories?
Do you know how I can leave? How do I wake up from this dream?
Time has no meaning here. No one can know when you will wake up, or if deeper slumber awaits.
Then... are you real, or a version from Qalaiša's memories?
It does not matter.
The life of Nezzsalem matters not. The name of Nezzsalem is synonymous with war.
But it has been a long time since I talked to someone like this.
Okay, maybe we can chat until I wake up...
Has the war for Londinium ended, Djall?
Yes... I suppose. But I don't think you'd like how it ended...
No...
Surely I would have passed on, but Kazdel and the Sarkaz will remain...
The Patriarch of the Nachzehrer stays silent for a long time, as though he has forgotten I am here.
A-Ahem!
Maybe we could, er, talk about something else?
Very well.
Is it turnip season?
Huh? Oh... not a lot of people plant them these days. Most people plant maize and sweet potatoes.
You're into food? I know a lot about that!
I mean... maybe not Nachzehrer... food...
...Go on.
I'm back.
I left a pot of herbal tea on the stove outside before I went out. It's just about ready now.
Get the tea set out, Nymph. The one with the pink roses is for you...
Nymph?!
The tray slips from the Northwind Witch's hand, and she only has time to grab the scalding teapot. The tray shatters on the floor, but she simply stares in horror at the table—
The garland of branches, thorns, and mossflower lies beneath the dim candlelight. The Djall is gesturing excitedly to it, as though roasting sweet potatoes in furnace ash.
It's even sweeter than honey when it's fresh out of the furnace! Worth the blisters it gives you.
Oh, look at me, I just keep talking about myself. What about you? What's your favorite food? Sauteed turnips?
Oh, sweet potatoes might be your next favorite? Just give me a minute, I'll fetch a few from the furnace for you.
Qalaiša! You're back! Hey, when did I wake up?
What's going on? Nezzsalem...?
Nymph stares blankly in front of her, then looks down at her hands, before looking to the Northwind Witch with teary eyes.
Q-Qalaiša! I'm sorry!
I'm sorry I'm sorry I'm sorry! I won't touch your stuff again!
Ouch! Hot! Why are you standing here with a teapot in your hands?
Why do you think?
How did you get to my unfinished trinket in the time it took for me to pick a few flowers from the garden...? You even got... him out.
Tell me what happened.
Th-The revenant detector started reacting as soon as I walked in. I felt like I had to look around a bit.
I never meant to put it on, but it's like my hands weren't my own.
Then I had a scary dream. One moment I was you, flying all over the battlefield. Then I was in an ice spring with you. Then a monster almost ate me!
Those are dreams that I made with my own memories to help Nachzehrer veterans cope after returning from war. They haven't been fully tested yet. They're dangerous!
Thank goodness you weren't hurt. I'm not leaving you alone in this house ever again, and you can forget about today's berry cookies, if you can't keep your hands to yourself!
I really didn't mean to put it on! But there was some sort of power compelling me. I thought you were playing a trick on me!
Could it be the revenant fragment you were talking about? No wonder the hut hasn't felt quite right these past few days. I thought it had indigestion or something...
The Northwind Witch puts down the teapot, walks around the room, then looks up at the center of the roof.
I gave you a choice, revered ancestor.
You can mock me with my memories and my house...
But if you disrespect my guest...
Shadows surge out from the corners of the house, as if wanting to devour the two.
But a pair of eyes, cold as ice, delivers its ultimatum.
Then...
The eyes close, and ice-blue witchcraft begins to spread through the space—
Are you gonna talk or not?
Stop! You're gonna twist my arm off.
Looks like you're the easiest of them to break. Good, saves me the effort.
One more time. Are you sure you didn't see it?
How was I supposed to see where your thing fell in the middle of the night?
Anything like that would make a bang when it crashed, but we didn't—
Thanks. See you later.
—Wait, I want to know what the *Sarkaz swearing* happened too!
Nymph? Are you okay? Nym—
Did you find your fragment?
I did!
The box closes with a click, and Nymph jumps down from the rubble, raising her handy box in triumph.
Aren't I awesome? I already have so many!
Yes, quite. I already had that fragment under my witchcraft but it was your 'awesome' arts that brought it down, along, with, my, house!
(Slow clap)
Sorry... that was the shadow I saw in my nightmare, and I lost control of my Arts because I was scared.
But I heard Nezzsalem say your hut was made out of Lich Arts? I happen to know a Lich...
(Gulps) P-Please, Qalaiša, why are you suddenly looking at me like that, you're scaring me...
What else did he tell you?
Funny you should bring that up, because I don't recall giving you permission to go over my memories.
I-It was an accident...
And you wasted perfectly good tea, and berry cookies...
Excuse me—what's going on, Nymph? Did you find the fragment?
You're a friend of Nymph's? Yes, she found her fragment, but I'm afraid she's busy at the moment.
You're... Madam Qalaiša, right?
So... what's she busy with?
She angered the ancestors and wrecked my house. So now she has to fix it before she can go.
But I thought your house walked into Kazdel and survived cannon blasts from bandits... Could Nymph really have wrecked it alone?
Em? What was that?
Oh, er, nothing, nothing at all, ma'am.
Would you like some tea? I poured it right before she blew the roof off.
Er, I appreciate the offer, but I'm good...
Keep at it, Nymph. Just a few more piles of rubble left and you're good to go.
I'm starving...
(Whimper) My berry cookies...
Another one. I wasn't even looking and I already spotted four safety hazards on my stroll...

Such a poorly designed junction of pipes is incredibly vulnerable to changes in the Furnace's power output. You're basically looking at a potential explosion.
Well, good thing the old timers in the furnace don't have to stay in there much longer. Hopefully, by then they'll...
Flower petals? Where did these come from?
Blue snowdrops, huh? I used to plant them back in Columbia. I didn't think they could grow in Kazdel.
Times sure are a-changing...
Beep—Beep—Beep—Beep—
What's that? A... Djall?
That's the guy! The detector is going nuts!
It's really loud. It's not going to explode, is it?
There must be a huge revenant fragment on this guy!
Stop pulling at my sleeve! It's a bespoke coat!
This guy-It's a living armor!
Living armor? Ha, haven't met such a rude little girl since the K'uk'ulkan.
He's starting to talk gibberish... the revenant must have possessed him. Pohl, hold the box for me!
You want to stuff the whole guy in? I don't think your box is big enough.
Here—goes!
Hey, get this box off my head before I lose my temper!
Huh? Why are you also in the box? And why are you in pieces?
He's making a run for it!
I'll tie his hands!
Stay put! Your big head's gonna break the box! I don't need Ermi getting mad at me again.
Let's get him to the furnace, quick.
Heheh.
They are a-changin' indeed.
Ermi Ermi, if you read me, please respond.
Ermi...? It's not working?
Young Djall, could you please unbind my hands? I promise I won't run. At least let me have a smoke?
No can do! I'm not letting you go until I get you back to the Furnace.
I told you, you've got the wrong guy. I've been meaning to drop by the Furnace, but I really didn't escape from the thing.
I have a Columbian ID in my shirt pocket. I could show you right now—if you unbind my hands.
It's a long trip from Columbia to Kazdel, isn't it?
If you'd told me you were from Rim Billiton, I probably would've believed you.
Ermi, can you hear me? Hey? Hello...?
The box's comm Arts structure has been ruined. You can slap it all you want, but that isn't going to fix it.
How dare you?! It's your fault my box got crushed in the first place!
If I fix your little box thing for you, will you free my hands?

Can you really fix it?
Don't you pull any tricks or anything! I-I have my own methods to deal with you!
I got it, little Djall. Relax. I'm not going anywhere.
Hold on. Are you actually buying this, Nymph? The guy gets possessed by a re—by the ancestors and you're ready to unbind him?
You sure love getting your word in, kiddo, but you don't seem to know a thing about revenants or Djaj. Djaj are the only ones that can't be influenced by revenants.
I'll free just one of your hands... Hey! How'd you break yourself out of that? You tricked me!
I knew you were up to no good!
I hope you can appreciate how much it hurts to have my hands bound for so long.
It hurts, even though you've got iron skin?
It's not iron. It's tin. And it doesn't exactly hurt. It's just...
Between his metallic fingers is a single cigarette; it seems to have appeared out of nowhere.
The odd man takes it to his mouth, takes a puff, then spins the cigarette around between his fingers before carefully smothering every spark of flame in his palms.
Huff—Ah, that's good.
You can't buy cigarettes this nice in Kazdel. I have to ration them.
Hey, you promised you'd fix my box.
I lied.
A Lich created the Arts in that box, and those prideful bastards never think about repairability when they design things. That's not to say none of them earned their pride, though.
Come on, you're just going back on your word!
Let me finish. You're looking for someone, aren't you? I can't fix the box, but I can help you find the Lich who made it.
H-How come you know who I'm looking for?
I don't, but I think I don't need to.
The strange man leans back as a puff of gray-whitish smoke gradually floats from his mouth.
The smoke disperses into a rippling cloud, like a stone being thrown into a pool of stagnant water. The revenant's trace fluctuations sweep across the area, drowning all of Kazdel in silence.
A brief moment later, the noise has reemerged, and the puff of smoke has completely dispersed.
Who are you exactly?
Just... who are you, mister?
They call me the Tin Man.
Tin Man...?
Look, the one you're looking for is here.
One can count all the revenants that walk the lands on one hand. Who might you be?
He says he's the Tin Man.
He says he's from Columbia.
...Kazdel is not ready for your return yet.
Hard to blame you. I never thought I'd ever come back here when I left.
What did you come back for?
He says he wants to go to the Furnace.
Nymphy!
Okay okay. I won't interrupt again. Go ahead and ask.
Then allow me to... Never mind. I suppose there's nothing else to ask.
Normally, a guest of your pedigree would be received by my master, but...
Your master? That stubborn old man? Let's not tell him, save my vacation from going completely down the drain.
If you'd like to visit and take a look beneath the Soul Furnace, I could bring you there, without telling anyone.
(Shakes head) Not beneath it. I want to go inside it.
Who do you want to visit there?
Who else?
Nymphy, have you gathered all the revenant fragments yet?
They're all inside my box. Here!
The young Lich nods and nonchalantly throws the slightly misshapen box into the shadows behind her.
Her gaze shifts back and forth between the Djall and the revenant, then finally lands on the Soul Furnace that stands in the distance.
Then let's go.
Nymphy, I'll find another time to give you your reward. And here's the surprise I promised you—Do you want to go into the Soul Furnace for a walk?
Can I really?!
Can I bring friends?! So many kind people have helped us along the way! I could never have collected all the fragments so quickly without them!
Ahem, to tell the truth, I've always wanted to go inside, ever since I was a kid, just to see how it works.
Well... As it stands, we've already broken multiple rules today. so...
Give me just a minute! I'll gather everybody up!
Everybody? Hold on...
Right, for my reward, can I get some scrap Oriron...? Pohl, help me explain it to Ermi, okay?
Uh, alright.
Ah, youth. So motivated. Already sprang away like an arrow from the bow.
I never introduced myself. I'm from down below, the slums, as people call it.
They call me the Consultant there, and I speak for them. Mr. Tin Man, and the Lich lady. Please, call me Pohl.
Don't call me a lady. Don't you think that's very improper?
Oh, kiddo... In certain ways, you're still far too young.
One, two, three, four... and me. Five! All of us are here!
Nymphy, the Furnace is a dangerous place. Why did you bring this many people? And that little girl behind you...
One child ticket, please.
This is not a sightseeing spot, and we don't sell tickets! No children, either!
Sorry, Cherie. I tried my best... Maybe next time?
There's no next time!
It's okay, Nymph. I was thinking that maybe the big furnace is full of flatbread like the little one we have. Turns out, it's all dark and dirty and lame!
What flatbread? We never bake anything with the furnace nodes! Your grandpa is still waiting outside, right? Quick, go find him!
There are still so many people here. Now what am I going to do with them?
They all helped me, a lot, and you promised me just now! They won't give you any trouble at all!
Everyone, I need all of you to give me your own words, not just Nymphy's.
It doesn't matter to me. All I want is to take a look at the work of that legendary designer. I've heard her signature is on the Soul Furnace's blueprints, too. Is that true?
Since when has the Soul Furnace had blueprints?
You can look all you want, but no touching. What about you, Gargoyle? What are you looking at?
If there's a wide enough pipeline that leads straight out of the city...
Gargoyles tend to be into complicated constructions like this. Don't worry. She's just checking whether the pipelines are sturdy enough.
Right. I'm trying to see if we can crawl through them.
What about you, Consultant? Trying to live your childhood dream? Like you said before?
(Shrugs) Is that not good enough of a reason?
Hmph.
I didn't expect to see you here, Qalaiša. What is a Nachzehrer who left her tribe doing here?
Left my tribe? No, we just hold different ideals.
As for what I am doing here... Hm, mainly, I couldn't bring myself to refuse such a sincere invitation from this cutie pie.
And if I manage to learn a few extraordinary witchcraft rituals while inside the Soul Furnace, that's all the better.
None of us here have any reason to lie. What's more, it was a Djall who invited all of us.
I've finished a whole cigarette already. Are you done with the roll call yet? I don't suppose anyone's going against their word?
Lich girl, try not to pick up your master's bad habits. Liches used to have a pretty great reputation, but now...
I'm not very well-versed in history, but I can tell you are making things up.
Okay, now. If everyone's ready, let's get going. Stay close. Don't fall behind.
Hooray! You're the best, Ermi! I knew you would be more amenable than those old, stuck-up fossils!
Turn right at the next corner.
There are potholes on the ground three steps ahead. Keep left.
You can open your eyes now. We're here.
So this is what it looks like inside! What's that faintly glowing thing?
The witchcraft core.
This is the deepest part of the Soul Furnace. As far as I know, ever since it was built, only Liches have come here to hold rituals to appease the revenants.
And the last ritual caused the big fireworks that led all of us here. Anything else to share?
Mr. Tin Man, should we proceed as we discussed?
Sure. I'll keep an eye out for you.
The fireworks were only an accident, and the ceremony wasn't completed.
My master tasked me with finding the revenant fragments scattered all over Kazdel, and I entrusted this mission to Nymph. Nymph then got your help. Which means...
My master owes all of you a favor. No, he owes all of us a favor.
As the keeper of the Temple of Knowledge, a favor of his is most valuable indeed. You could accomplish quite a lot with it.
But, as his student, I've already decided in his place to use this favor to do something exceedingly difficult.
I'm kinda getting the feeling you're about to say something horrible... Is it okay if I leave now?
No way! I invited you here. If anyone's leaving, I'm leaving first!
Ahem, it's actually nothing to be scared of. She's just being sulky that she got taught a lesson by the stubborn old man, and she's trying to win back some face.
What face?
The revenant's... face. I'll finish my master's appeasement ceremony without a flaw. So, are you in?
Yaaahhh!
Sorry, I couldn't stand that awkward silence, so I thought I'd make some noise.
Uh, like I said, I'm just here to admire that legendary designer's work... Since I've now seen it, I'll leave this revenant business to the rest of you.
We kept our eyes closed the whole time, and you're telling me you're already done?!
If it was just watching the ceremony, I'd be totally fine, but the Lich here seems to want us to be active participants?
If we're dealing with revenants, should we let Amiya know? And the Babel members, too?
All of us have seen how powerful the revenants can be. Even little fragments were causing chaos all over Kazdel.
Pohl, stop sweating! It's not that bad!
Not that bad? Think about it, Nymph. If it'd taken us just a little longer to get to any one of the fragments, what do you think would have happened?
There would've been bloodshed if those gangs had drawn their blades, and that berserk colossus wouldn't have stopped its fist...
And think about the risks when facing the revenants who have been burning in the Furnace for centuries. Is that really something we can take by ourselves?
Yes.
Like I said, I'll keep an eye out for you.
A revenant. A powerful revenant who turned his back on his homeland, only to return as if nothing had happened.
I have no qualms with this.
I'm fine with it, too! What's there to be scared of if we have such a bad-ass ancestor looking after us?
You're getting ahead of yourself, Nymph. Even with Mr. Tin Man here, it's not going to be easy to appease the ancestors with just the few of us.
Why do you think it'll be hard? We've met so many people on our way here. Everyone may live pretty hard lives, but we're all living life to the fullest.
Maybe the ceremony failed last time because the ancestors had heard enough nagging. I mean, when all you ever hear is the future and sacrifice, blahblahblah, how is anyone supposed to cheer up?
If we tell them about how people in Kazdel live these days, that might drive up their enthusiasm!
Besides, the revenants might still remember that legendary designer, they might know her true identity...
That sounds splendid. Count me in!
We've made it this far. We can't go back empty-handed. And if the revenants can show us a secret passageway out of the city...
Then you're in, too! Pohl, what do you say? Still hesitating?
It's a bit of a risk, but I can't think of a reason to not go for it. I'm in.
Okay, then let's throw the ancestors an appeasement ceremony like they've never seen! What do you think, Mr. Tin Man?
I'm intrigued. I said yes to the Lich girl there because she said basically what you just did.
Huh? Really, Ermi?
We're the ones who will walk the path of Kazdel's future, not them, so...
Of course, we have the right to do this.
Now cover your ears. I'll begin the incantations.
Let's try again. Relax... Flawed souls, come near, and heed my call.
In the flames... as your bloodlines melt away... heed my call.
What's going on? There's no response at all.
Mr. Tin Man, are you still there?
Maybe I've gone too deep... I'm not getting any response. Did I do something wrong?
No, if I turn back now, I'll be admitting to a failure before it's even over.
In the shapeless shadows, spots of cold light revolve around my consciousness, leaving layered trails behind them.
Once you try to understand the logic within, you will immediately lose yourself in it. Stop. Think, think...
These... are the revenants' fragments. I remember now. I try bringing them together, but they keep escaping through my fingers, like stardust.
They are talking. There's one... two. A lot of voices slowly drawing near me. The coldness fades away, and the frigid ice burns bright. I can feel it—
Agitation, hatred, chaos... It's more than them not hearing my cries, they... are simply ignoring me. Ultimately, they are annoyed.
They want to... tear me apart.
Step back... Three, two, one. Cut your senses off and step back!
Ermi, are you okay?! You were standing there, shaking. That was scary!
I... I'm alright. Where is Mr. Tin Man?
He's over there by the wall. He hasn't moved for a while now with his head down, and I'm not sure what's going on with him, or if those metallic joints ever ache.
The ceremony... did it work?
I was returning the fragments to the revenants, but I almost lost myself in their consciousness. It was the Tin Man who saved me.
Not a big deal. I just offered a hand. You could've handled it by yourself, although it would have taken more time.
He was standing still the whole time, why this sound feels like it's echoing inside my head?
It's not a bad thing for kids to have that kind of drive, but you went too deep. Good thing you had an old man like me to watch you from ashore and occasionally drop the odd pearl.
But the revenants didn't heed my call.
I half expected as much. Having spent so many years in the Furnace, their brains have been torched. No, they haven't had brains in a long time.
You, the schemy kid. Yes, you. Give me a hand. Take the notepad from my coat's inside pocket, and a few pens, too.
You aren't going to suddenly wake up and grab my hand, right? Like that kind of thing in the horror stories.
I'm not that cliche. Once you have the notebook, write down whatever you want to say to the revenants, tear the pages out, and burn them.
The lighter's in my pants pocket. Or use Arts. Either way.
Will that really reach them?
They can't make me look bad, right?
Write down whatever you want, but I suggest topics they might be interested in—Hey, what do you folks want to know about?
Things about Kazdel. Write them down, tear the pages out, burn them.
What are you doing right now...?
I ran into an old friend. We're catching up. Don't worry about me.
I'm finished! Ermi, make me a fireball, will you?
Can't you manage simple Arts like that...? Never mind. Who else has something ready to burn?
No need. I found the lighter.
Nymph, what did you write?
Not telling. Wishes don't come true if you say them out loud.
You aren't making a wish!
Can you feel it? The Furnace seems to be shaking... I can see it!
The ancestors have manifested!

lines.st: Time to talk about you.

lines.x: Me? Actually, he came to me back then too, but I didn't go with him.
I saw the bones on him, clattering away. Were you in there?

lines.st: Don't remember.
Memories don't do anyone any good here other than... adding to the suffering.

lines.x: But you still recognized me.

lines.st: Because I loathe you. We loathe you.
Our resentment fanned the flames... It's what he wished.

lines.x: You lot certainly have a reason to loathe. I find myself with hard feelings every now and then, too.

lines.st: You should never have returned. Why are you here?

lines.x: Let's just say I'm here to see old friends. I've spent too much time in Columbia, waking up every morning to see a bunch of newly invented gadgets. But sometimes, I reminisce on old times and old friends.
That's why I decided to take a vacation and came back here. It's been centuries, and I heard Kazdel got rebuilt again. I was curious what the place would look like.
I heard that they'd be renovating this Furnace soon and there would be a celebration to release all of you. Haha, though I wonder if you'll last that long.

lines.st: It's more of a... funeral. An endless funeral.

lines.x: I'm aware.
Revenants. All of you died long ago, but you need a funeral nonetheless.
I understand that. I do...
In some parts of Columbia, you tell jokes about the dead at funerals. Everybody laughs and forgets the teary parts. I think that's a pretty good tradition. More places should have funerals like that.
I just so happened to have met some interesting kids, and they've got some interesting ideas. Why not hear what they have to say? Think of it as a rehearsal for your funeral.
Try not to scare them, though. They... They're all good Kazdelian kids.
It's only been a second. Why are my legs so stiff? Seems I need to find some time for a spa treatment on my joints.
Hm? Where's my lighter?
The schemy boy took it.
Can you feel it? The Furnace seems to be shaking... I can see it!
The ancestors have manifested!
Nymph! Watch out! Come over here.

lines.st: You think you have the right to stand before the revenants and talk about the future of Kazdel?

lines.x: Not only the future... What I wrote is probably more like legends and folktales?

Those mercenaries sounded pretty confident, and I wasn't sure if it was true, so I figured I would ask you if there was ever really a Kazdel city that fell straight to the ground, leaving only a big pit?

lines.st: You would curse Kazdel to be buried in the earth over a mere squabble?

lines.x: Curse? Perish the thought. If such a place actually exists, I want to see it. I might just find some lost treasures I could use to expand my style.
I've already thought up the exhibition's title. 'The Lost Kazdel: Echoes of Eras Past'. Sounds classy, don't you think?

There's been so many Kazdels over the course of history. Maybe one of them really did come crashing down...
Uh, I'm just running my mouth. Don't get mad at me.
What I wrote is my hopes for Kazdel's future, nothing too wild.
Really? Let's see... 'In a New Kazdel, the Sarkaz help one another, and outsiders no longer have to crowd on the city outskirts.'
'People come here for better lives regardless of race, and there is no Royal Court anymore.' Pohl, did you write that?
Th-That's mine! Didn't I burn it? How did you find it?
Regardless of race... No Royal Court...
Can you see what I wrote?
Yes. Well... Your vision is pretty bold, Gargoyle. I'm afraid it may never come true.
A lot of people said the same thing a lot of years ago, to another Gargoyle.
(Whispers) Ermi, Ermi. Which step of the ceremony are we on?
We're not on any step. Ever since the ancestors showed up, I've completely lost control of this ceremony.
Right now, the feeling I'm getting is that the ancestors are toying with us.
Toying?
They seem to enjoy hearing a different kind of stories.
Crashing to the earth, flying into the sky, no Royal Court, and an entire city turned to revenants... You people come up with some wild ideas. Do you really think—

lines.st: Do you really think that, compared to the way it is now, Kazdel would be better like that?
If Kazdel makes its way toward one of them, only to find it's the wrong path, what will you do?

lines.x: There's no such thing as 'the wrong path'.
Nobody knows what form Kazdel will ultimately take, but all of us want the people here to live better lives.
If life gets better, how could you call it the wrong path?
Even if the path we end up taking is a treacherous one, it would be better than finding ourselves at a dead end, wouldn't it?
There is no response from the furnace core. The Tin Man looks down once again, all while his finger continues to rhythmically tap his other arm.
Nymph feels her heart beating faster, as the staff in her hand gets hotter.
Come on, hurry up and say something. Nymph glances at Crownie to plead for help, but she sees only a dark shadow in the distance.

lines.st: If you think Kazdel would be better that way, then try it for yourselves.
You will prove for yourselves whether the outcome is good or bad, and you will bear any consequences.
Let's see if you truly have the right to find Kazdel... a different path.

lines.x: A different path... to where?

lines.st: To the stories you just told.
